     2→"""
     3→flockdesk.shared.schemas.user_profile
     4→====================================
     5→
     6→Strongly-typed, versioned schema for persisting a user’s profile in FlockDesk.
     7→The schema is expressed as nested Pydantic models to guarantee integrity,
     8→provide automatic (de)serialization, and enable painless migrations when the
     9→structure evolves.
    10→
    11→The profile encapsulates:
    12→    • Identity   – id, e-mail address, display name, avatar path
    13→    • Preferences – theme, shortcuts, window layout, enabled plug-ins
    14→    • Timestamps  – creation, last update, schema revision
    15→
    16→The helper methods `save()` and `load()` make it trivial to persist and restore
    17→profiles as UTF-8 JSON files.  When older documents are encountered, an
    18→internal migration pipeline `_migrate()` upgrades them on-the-fly so the rest
    19→of the application can rely on the latest shape.
    20→
    21→This module has *no* external runtime dependencies outside of `pydantic`,
    22→`uuid`, and the Python standard library.
    23→"""
    24→
    25→from __future__ import annotations
    26→
    27→import json
    28→from datetime import datetime
    29→from pathlib import Path
    30→from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
    31→from uuid import UUID, uuid4
    32→
    33→from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, ValidationError, root_validator, validator
    34→
    35→__all__ = [
    36→    "ThemeSettings",
    37→    "ShortcutBinding",
    38→    "KeyboardPreferences",
    39→    "WindowGeometry",
    40→    "WindowLayout",
    41→    "PluginConfig",
    42→    "UserPreferences",
    43→    "UserProfile",
    44→]
    45→
    46→###############################################################################
    47→# Low-level building blocks
    48→###############################################################################
    49→
    50→
    51→class ThemeSettings(BaseModel):
    52→    """
    53→    Visual styling preferences.
    54→
    55→    Attributes
    56→    ----------
    57→    name
    58→        Human-readable name of the theme.
    59→    accent_color
    60→        Hex triplet to use for accent highlights (validated).
    61→    """
    62→
    63→    name: str = Field("FlockDark", description="Name of the currently used theme.")
    64→    accent_color: str = Field(
    65→        "#1abc9c",
    66→        regex=r"^#(?:[0-9a-fA-F]{3}){1,2}$",
    67→        description="HEX RGB color (e.g. #RRGGBB) used for accent highlights.",
    68→    )
    69→
    70→
    71→class ShortcutBinding(BaseModel):
    72→    """
    73→    Represents one keyboard shortcut mapping.
    74→
    75→    The `keys` field accepts multi-stroke definitions, e.g. `['Ctrl+Shift+P']`.
    76→    """
    77→
    78→    action: str = Field(..., description="Canonical action identifier.")
    79→    keys: List[str] = Field(
    80→        ...,
    81→        min_items=1,
    82→        description="Key sequence(s) that trigger the action.",
    83→    )
    84→
    85→
    86→class KeyboardPreferences(BaseModel):
    87→    """Collection of user-defined keyboard shortcuts."""
    88→
    89→    bindings: List[ShortcutBinding] = Field(
    90→        default_factory=list, description="All custom keyboard bindings."
    91→    )
    92→
    93→    def get_binding_for_action(self, action: str) -> Optional[ShortcutBinding]:
    94→        """
    95→        Return the shortcut binding for a given action, if present.
    96→        """
    97→        return next((b for b in self.bindings if b.action == action), None)
    98→
    99→
   100→class WindowGeometry(BaseModel):
   101→    """
   102→    Geometry description for a single window or dock widget.
   103→    """
   104→
   105→    x: int
   106→    y: int
   107→    width: int
   108→    height: int
   109→    is_maximized: bool = Field(
   110→        False, description="Whether the window was maximised when saved."
   111→    )
   112→
   113→    @validator("*", pre=True)
   114→    def _ensure_int(cls, v: Any) -> int:
   115→        """
   116→        Pydantic sometimes passes `None` for missing fields, we make sure the
   117→        geometry is always an integer ≥ 0 to avoid Qt warnings later.
   118→        """
   119→        if not isinstance(v, int) or v < 0:
   120→            raise ValueError("Window geometry values must be non-negative integers.")
   121→        return v
   122→
   123→
   124→class WindowLayout(BaseModel):
   125→    """
   126→    Persisted layout for main and dock windows.
   127→    """
   128→
   129→    main_window: WindowGeometry
   130→    dock_widgets: Dict[str, WindowGeometry] = Field(
   131→        default_factory=dict,
   132→        description="Mapping dock-widget-id -> geometry.",
   133→    )
   134→
   135→
   136→class PluginConfig(BaseModel):
   137→    """
   138→    Per-plug-in runtime state (enabled, custom settings, …).
   139→    """
   140→
   141→    id: str = Field(..., description="Fully-qualified plug-in identifier.")
   142→    enabled: bool = Field(True, description="Is the plug-in enabled?")
   143→    state: Dict[str, Any] = Field(
   144→        default_factory=dict,
   145→        description="Opaque blob saved/restored by the plug-in itself.",
   146→    )
   147→
   148→
   149→###############################################################################
   150→# High-level aggregated preferences
   151→###############################################################################
   152→
   153→
   154→class UserPreferences(BaseModel):
   155→    """
   156→    Root container for everything customisable by the user.
   157→    """
   158→
   159→    theme: ThemeSettings = Field(default_factory=ThemeSettings)
   160→    keyboard: KeyboardPreferences = Field(default_factory=KeyboardPreferences)
   161→    layout: WindowLayout
   162→    plugins: List[PluginConfig] = Field(default_factory=list)
   163→    last_updated: datetime = Field(
   164→        default_factory=datetime.utcnow,
   165→        description="UTC timestamp when preferences changed last.",
   166→    )
   167→
   168→    # Convenience helpers -----------------------------------------------------
   169→
   170→    def plugin(self, plugin_id: str) -> Optional[PluginConfig]:
   171→        """
   172→        Fetch a plug-in configuration by id.
   173→        """
   174→        return next((p for p in self.plugins if p.id == plugin_id), None)
   175→
   176→    def enable_plugin(self, plugin_id: str) -> None:
   177→        """
   178→        Enable (or add) a plug-in.
   179→        """
   180→        cfg = self.plugin(plugin_id)
   181→        if cfg:
   182→            cfg.enabled = True
   183→        else:
   184→            self.plugins.append(PluginConfig(id=plugin_id, enabled=True))
   185→
   186→    def disable_plugin(self, plugin_id: str) -> None:
   187→        """
   188→        Disable a plug-in if it exists.
   189→        """
   190→        cfg = self.plugin(plugin_id)
   191→        if cfg:
   192→            cfg.enabled = False
   193→
   194→
   195→###############################################################################
   196→# Top-level profile
   197→###############################################################################
   198→
   199→
   200→class UserProfile(BaseModel):
   201→    """
   202→    Complete persisted user profile.
   203→
   204→    The model is intentionally *not* immutable – live components mutate it, and
   205→    we rely on Pydantic’s `validate_assignment` to keep things consistent.
   206→    """
   207→
   208→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- Meta
   209→    schema_version: int = Field(
   210→        1,
   211→        const=True,
   212→        description="Monotonically increasing schema revision.",
   213→    )
   214→
   215→    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- Identity
   216→    user_id: UUID = Field(default_factory=uuid4)
   217→    email: str
   218→    display_name: str
   219→    avatar_path: Optional[str] = Field(
   220→        None,
   221→        description="File path or HTTPS URL to the avatar image.",
   222→    )
   223→
   224→    # -------------------------------------------------------------- Preferences
   225→    preferences: UserPreferences = Field(
   226→        default_factory=lambda: UserPreferences(
   227→            layout=WindowLayout(
   228→                main_window=WindowGeometry(
   229→                    x=50,
   230→                    y=50,
   231→                    width=1280,
   232→                    height=800,
   233→                    is_maximized=False,
   234→                )
   235→            )
   236→        )
   237→    )
   238→
   239→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------- Audit
   240→    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)
   241→    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)
   242→
   243→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ Config
   244→    class Config:
   245→        validate_assignment = True
   246→        json_encoders = {datetime: lambda dt: dt.replace(tzinfo=None).isoformat()}
   247→
   248→    # ----------------------------------------------------------------- Hooks
   249→    @validator("email")
   250→    def _email_must_be_valid(cls, v: str) -> str:
   251→        if "@" not in v:
   252→            raise ValueError("Invalid e-mail address.")
   253→        return v.strip().lower()
   254→
   255→    @root_validator
   256→    def _touch_updated_at(cls, values: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   257→        """
   258→        Whenever the model is (re-)validated we update the timestamp so callers
   259→        don’t have to remember doing it themselves.
   260→        """
   261→        values["updated_at"] = datetime.utcnow()
   262→        return values
   263→
   264→    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- Public
   265→    # Persistence helpers .....................................................
   266→
   267→    def save(self, path: str | Path) -> None:
   268→        """
   269→        Flush the profile to *path* as pretty-printed UTF-8 JSON.
   270→        """
   271→        path = Path(path)
   272→        try:
   273→            path.write_text(self.json(indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding="utf-8")
   274→        except OSError as exc:  # pragma: no cover
   275→            raise IOError(f"Unable to write user profile to {path}: {exc}") from exc
   276→
   277→    @classmethod
   278→    def load(cls, path: str | Path) -> "UserProfile":
   279→        """
   280→        Load a profile from *path*, migrating older versions automatically.
   281→        """
   282→        path = Path(path)
   283→        if not path.exists():
   284→            raise FileNotFoundError(f"User profile not found: {path}")
   285→
   286→        raw_json = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
   287→        data = json.loads(raw_json)
   288→
   289→        current_version = cls.__fields__["schema_version"].default
   290→        stored_version = data.get("schema_version", 0)
   291→        if stored_version != current_version:
   292→            data = cls._migrate(data, from_version=stored_version)
   293→
   294→        try:
   295→            return cls.parse_obj(data)
   296→        except ValidationError as exc:  # pragma: no cover
   297→            raise ValueError(
   298→                f"Corrupted profile '{path}':\n{exc}"
   299→            ) from exc
   300→
   301→    # Migration pipeline ......................................................
   302→
   303→    @staticmethod
   304→    def _migrate(payload: Dict[str, Any], *, from_version: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   305→        """
   306→        Upgrade *payload* to the latest schema version.
   307→
   308→        Every `if` block is responsible for migrating *from* a specific older
   309→        revision *to* the immediate next one.
   310→        """
   311→        migrated = dict(payload)  # shallow copy
   312→
   313→        if from_version < 1:
   314→            # ––– v0 ➜ v1 ––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––
   315→            #  • Ensure `display_name` exists (fallback to local-part of e-mail)
   316→            #
   317→            migrated.setdefault(
   318→                "display_name",
   319→                migrated.get("email", "User").split("@")[0].title(),
   320→            )
   321→            migrated["schema_version"] = 1
   322→
   323→        return migrated
   324→
   325→    # Convenience API .........................................................
   326→
   327→    def update_preferences(self, **changes: Any) -> None:
   328→        """
   329→        Patch top-level keys of :class:`UserPreferences`.
   330→
   331→        Examples
   332→        --------
   333→        >>> user.update_preferences(theme=ThemeSettings(name="Solarized"))
   334→        """
   335→        for key, value in changes.items():
   336→            if not hasattr(self.preferences, key):
   337→                raise AttributeError(f"Unknown preference section: {key!r}")
   338→            setattr(self.preferences, key, value)
   339→
   340→        # Re-validate to bump `updated_at`
   341→        self.__dict__["updated_at"] = datetime.utcnow()
   342→        self.preferences.last_updated = self.updated_at
   343→```

     2→"""
     3→flockdesk.modules.presence.model.user_status
     4→===========================================
     5→
     6→Model abstractions that represent a user’s *presence* state inside FlockDesk.
     7→The model is intentionally agnostic of the underlying transport/event-bus
     8→implementation so it can be reused on the server as well as within micro-UIs.
     9→
    10→The responsibilities of this module are:
    11→
    12→    • Offer a strict, exhaustive enumeration of valid presence states
    13→    • Provide a value-object (`UserStatus`) that encapsulates all status-related
    14→      metadata (state, message, timestamps, …)
    15→    • Enforce *legal* state-transitions via a tiny state-machine
    16→    • Offer (de)serialization helpers for round-tripping over the event-bus
    17→    • Provide an Observer API so interested parties can subscribe to status
    18→      changes in a decoupled fashion
    19→
    20→The code purposefully stays dependency-light (only stdlib) to keep the model
    21→portable across the frontend and backend code-bases.
    22→"""
    23→
    24→from __future__ import annotations
    25→
    26→import enum
    27→import json
    28→import logging
    29→import threading
    30→from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field
    31→from datetime import datetime, timezone
    32→from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, Final, Iterable, List, Mapping, MutableSet
    33→
    34→__all__ = [
    35→    "PresenceState",
    36→    "IllegalStatusTransition",
    37→    "UserStatus",
    38→]
    39→
    40→_LOGGER: Final = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    41→
    42→
    43→class PresenceState(enum.Enum):
    44→    """
    45→    Exhaustive list of presence states a user can be in.
    46→    """
    47→
    48→    OFFLINE = "offline"          # The user is not connected
    49→    INVISIBLE = "invisible"      # Connected, but appears offline
    50→    ONLINE = "online"            # Active and available
    51→    AWAY = "away"                # Idle or temporarily away
    52→    DND = "do_not_disturb"       # Does not want to be interrupted
    53→    BUSY = "busy"                # Actively working, but may respond
    54→    PRESENTING = "presenting"    # Sharing screen / in focus-mode
    55→
    56→    @classmethod
    57→    def from_str(cls, value: str) -> "PresenceState":
    58→        """
    59→        Convert a string to a PresenceState. Case-insensitive.
    60→        """
    61→        try:
    62→            return cls(value.lower())
    63→        except ValueError as exc:
    64→            raise ValueError(f"Unknown presence state: {value!r}") from exc
    65→
    66→
    67→# Legal state transitions ----------------------------------------------------
    68→
    69→
    70→_ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS: Mapping[PresenceState, MutableSet[PresenceState]] = {
    71→    PresenceState.OFFLINE: {
    72→        PresenceState.ONLINE,
    73→        PresenceState.INVISIBLE,
    74→    },
    75→    PresenceState.INVISIBLE: {
    76→        PresenceState.ONLINE,
    77→        PresenceState.DND,
    78→        PresenceState.AWAY,
    79→        PresenceState.OFFLINE,
    80→    },
    81→    PresenceState.ONLINE: {
    82→        PresenceState.AWAY,
    83→        PresenceState.BUSY,
    84→        PresenceState.DND,
    85→        PresenceState.PRESENTING,
    86→        PresenceState.INVISIBLE,
    87→        PresenceState.OFFLINE,
    88→    },
    89→    PresenceState.AWAY: {
    90→        PresenceState.ONLINE,
    91→        PresenceState.OFFLINE,
    92→        PresenceState.INVISIBLE,
    93→    },
    94→    PresenceState.BUSY: {
    95→        PresenceState.ONLINE,
    96→        PresenceState.DND,
    97→        PresenceState.OFFLINE,
    98→    },
    99→    PresenceState.DND: {
   100→        PresenceState.ONLINE,
   101→        PresenceState.OFFLINE,
   102→    },
   103→    PresenceState.PRESENTING: {
   104→        PresenceState.ONLINE,
   105→        PresenceState.OFFLINE,
   106→    },
   107→}
   108→
   109→
   110→class IllegalStatusTransition(RuntimeError):
   111→    """
   112→    Raised when an attempt is made to switch to a state that is not legal.
   113→    """
   114→
   115→
   116→# Observer pattern helpers ----------------------------------------------------
   117→
   118→
   119→Observer = Callable[["UserStatus"], None]
   120→
   121→
   122→@dataclass(slots=True, frozen=True)
   123→class _Metadata:
   124→    """Container for fields we do *not* expose via asdict()."""
   125→
   126→    sequence: int = 0
   127→
   128→
   129→# Main model ------------------------------------------------------------------
   130→
   131→
   132→@dataclass(slots=True)
   133→class UserStatus:
   134→    """
   135→    Value-object carrying the status of a single user.
   136→
   137→    The object is *mutable* so that UI components can hold a shared reference
   138→    that reflects live changes. Thread-safety is guaranteed via an internal
   139→    re-entrant lock.
   140→    """
   141→
   142→    user_id: str
   143→    state: PresenceState = PresenceState.OFFLINE
   144→    message: str = ""
   145→    last_changed: datetime = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc))
   146→
   147→    # Private fields ----------------------------------------------------------
   148→    _observers: List[Observer] = field(default_factory=list, init=False, repr=False)
   149→    _lock: threading.RLock = field(default_factory=threading.RLock, init=False, repr=False)
   150→    _meta: _Metadata = field(default_factory=_Metadata, init=False, repr=False)
   151→
   152→    # Public API --------------------------------------------------------------
   153→
   154→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   155→    # Status mutation                                                        #
   156→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   157→
   158→    def update(
   159→        self,
   160→        new_state: PresenceState,
   161→        /,
   162→        *,
   163→        message: str | None = None,
   164→        force: bool = False,
   165→    ) -> None:
   166→        """
   167→        Transition to *new_state*.
   168→
   169→        Parameters
   170→        ----------
   171→        new_state:
   172→            The desired presence state.
   173→        message:
   174→            Optional user-visible status-message (e.g. “Grabbing lunch”). ``None``
   175→            keeps the current message untouched, an *empty* string clears it.
   176→        force:
   177→            Skip the allowed-transition validation. Reserved for system-level
   178→            code (e.g. session-restore) that needs to restore an older snapshot
   179→            verbatim.
   180→        """
   181→        with self._lock:
   182→            if not force and not self._is_transition_allowed(new_state):
   183→                raise IllegalStatusTransition(
   184→                    f"{self.state.value} ➔ {new_state.value} is not allowed."
   185→                )
   186→
   187→            self.state = new_state
   188→            if message is not None:
   189→                self.message = message
   190→
   191→            self.last_changed = datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc)
   192→            object.__setattr__(
   193→                self._meta,
   194→                "sequence",
   195→                self._meta.sequence + 1,
   196→            )
   197→
   198→        _LOGGER.debug(
   199→            "User '%s' changed status to %s (seq=%d)",
   200→            self.user_id,
   201→            new_state.value,
   202→            self._meta.sequence,
   203→        )
   204→        self._notify_observers()
   205→
   206→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   207→    # Observer utilities                                                    #
   208→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   209→
   210→    def attach(self, cb: Observer, /) -> None:
   211→        """
   212→        Subscribe *cb* to future status changes. Duplicate registrations
   213→        are ignored.
   214→        """
   215→        with self._lock:
   216→            if cb not in self._observers:
   217→                self._observers.append(cb)
   218→
   219→    def detach(self, cb: Observer, /) -> None:
   220→        """
   221→        Unsubscribe *cb*. Calls to unknown observers are ignored.
   222→        """
   223→        with self._lock:
   224→            try:
   225→                self._observers.remove(cb)
   226→            except ValueError:
   227→                pass
   228→
   229→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   230→    # Serialization                                                         #
   231→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   232→
   233→    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   234→        """
   235→        Serialize the object into a JSON-compatible dict.
   236→        """
   237→        with self._lock:
   238→            payload = asdict(self)
   239→            # Replace complex objects
   240→            payload["state"] = self.state.value
   241→            payload["last_changed"] = self.last_changed.isoformat()
   242→            # Strip private fields added by dataclasses.asdict()
   243→            payload.pop("_observers", None)
   244→            payload.pop("_lock", None)
   245→            payload.pop("_meta", None)
   246→            payload["sequence"] = self._meta.sequence
   247→            return payload
   248→
   249→    def to_json(self, *, pretty: bool = False) -> str:
   250→        """
   251→        Serialize the object to a JSON string.
   252→        """
   253→        if pretty:
   254→            return json.dumps(self.to_dict(), indent=2, sort_keys=True)
   255→        return json.dumps(self.to_dict(), separators=(",", ":"))
   256→
   257→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   258→    # Static helpers                                                        #
   259→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   260→
   261→    @classmethod
   262→    def from_dict(cls, data: Mapping[str, Any], /) -> "UserStatus":
   263→        """
   264→        Restore a *UserStatus* from the mapping created by :py:meth:`to_dict`.
   265→        """
   266→        try:
   267→            instance = cls(
   268→                user_id=str(data["user_id"]),
   269→                state=PresenceState.from_str(data["state"]),
   270→                message=data.get("message", ""),
   271→                last_changed=datetime.fromisoformat(data["last_changed"]),
   272→            )
   273→        except KeyError as exc:
   274→            raise ValueError("Malformed user-status payload (missing field)") from exc
   275→
   276→        # Restore the monotonically increasing sequence number (private)
   277→        object.__setattr__(instance._meta, "sequence", int(data.get("sequence", 0)))
   278→        return instance
   279→
   280→    @classmethod
   281→    def from_json(cls, raw: str | bytes, /) -> "UserStatus":
   282→        """
   283→        Restore a *UserStatus* from its JSON representation.
   284→        """
   285→        return cls.from_dict(json.loads(raw))
   286→
   287→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   288→    # Misc. utilities                                                       #
   289→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   290→
   291→    def copy(self) -> "UserStatus":
   292→        """
   293→        Return a deep copy that is detached from observers and locks.
   294→        """
   295→        return UserStatus.from_dict(self.to_dict())
   296→
   297→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   298→    # Private helpers                                                       #
   299→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   300→
   301→    def _is_transition_allowed(self, new_state: PresenceState) -> bool:
   302→        return new_state in _ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS[self.state]
   303→
   304→    def _notify_observers(self) -> None:
   305→        """
   306→        Notify subscribers in a *thread-safe* manner – but call them *outside*
   307→        the lock to avoid re-entrancy deadlocks. A failing observer will be
   308→        logged and *detached* automatically so that one bad plugin does not
   309→        block further updates.
   310→        """
   311→        # Snapshot list to keep iteration stable even when observers detach
   312→        with self._lock:
   313→            observers: Iterable[Observer] = tuple(self._observers)
   314→
   315→        for cb in observers:
   316→            try:
   317→                cb(self)
   318→            except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except
   319→                _LOGGER.exception(
   320→                    "Observer %r failed while processing status-change for '%s'. "
   321→                    "Observer has been removed.",
   322→                    cb,
   323→                    self.user_id,
   324→                )
   325→                self.detach(cb)

     2→"""
     3→flockdesk.core.app
     4→==================
     5→
     6→The central orchestration runtime for the FlockDesk desktop suite.
     7→Responsible for:
     8→
     9→* Application-wide lifecycle & graceful shutdown

    11→* Plugin discovery, dependency wiring and hot-reload hooks
    12→* Persisted user settings & dynamic preference updates

    15→"""
    16→
    17→from __future__ import annotations
    18→
    19→import asyncio
    20→import importlib
    21→import importlib.util
    22→import json
    23→import logging
    24→import os

    28→from pathlib import Path
    29→from types import ModuleType
    30→from typing import (
    31→    Any,
    32→    Awaitable,
    33→    Callable,
    34→    Coroutine,
    35→    Dict,
    36→    Iterable,
    37→    List,

    83→class Event:
    84→    """
    85→    A lightweight event envelope exchanged over the FlockDesk event bus.
    86→    """
    87→
    88→    __slots__ = ("topic", "payload", "meta")
    89→
    90→    def __init__(self, topic: str, payload: Any = None, **meta: Any) -> None:
    91→        self.topic: str = topic
    92→        self.payload: Any = payload
    93→        self.meta: Dict[str, Any] = meta

   102→Listener = Callable[[Event], Union[None, Awaitable[None]]]

   105→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   106→# Event Bus Implementation                                                   #
   107→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   108→class EventBus:
   109→    """
   110→    A minimal in-process publish/subscribe mechanism.
   111→
   112→    The bus is intentionally *naïve* but good enough for inter-plugin traffic.
   113→    More advanced requirements (wildcards, QoS, type routing) can be layered
   114→    on top by dedicated broker services.
   115→    """
   116→
   117→    def __init__(self) -> None:
   118→        self._subscribers: Dict[str, Set[Listener]] = {}
   119→        self._loop: asyncio.AbstractEventLoop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
   120→
   121→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   122→    # Subscription                                                          #
   123→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   124→    def subscribe(self, topic: str, listener: Listener) -> None:
   125→        listeners = self._subscribers.setdefault(topic, set())
   126→        listeners.add(listener)
   127→        logger.debug("Listener %s subscribed to topic %s", listener, topic)
   128→
   129→    def unsubscribe(self, topic: str, listener: Listener) -> None:
   130→        try:
   131→            self._subscribers[topic].discard(listener)
   132→            logger.debug("Listener %s unsubscribed from topic %s", listener, topic)
   133→        except KeyError:
   134→            logger.warning("Attempted to unsubscribe unknown topic %s", topic)
   135→
   136→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   137→    # Publishing                                                            #
   138→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   139→    def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Any = None, **meta: Any) -> None:
   140→        """
   141→        Schedule the dispatch of an event to all interested listeners.
   142→        """
   143→        event = Event(topic, payload, **meta)
   144→        logger.debug("Publishing event %s", event)
   145→        listeners = self._subscribers.get(topic, set()).copy()
   146→
   147→        for listener in listeners:
   148→            try:
   149→                result = listener(event)
   150→                if asyncio.iscoroutine(result):
   151→                    # Fire-and-forget, but capture exceptions.
   152→                    task = self._loop.create_task(result)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
   153→                    task.add_done_callback(self._capture_async_exception)
   154→            except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except
   155→                logger.exception(
   156→                    "Synchronous listener %s crashed while handling %s: %s",
   157→                    listener,
   158→                    topic,
   159→                    exc,
   160→                )
   161→
   162→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   163→    # Helpers                                                               #
   164→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   165→    def _capture_async_exception(self, task: asyncio.Task[Any]) -> None:  # noqa: D401
   166→        """
   167→        Funnel unhandled exceptions from background tasks into the logger.
   168→        """
   169→        if task.cancelled():
   170→            return
   171→        if exc := task.exception():
   172→            logger.exception("Async listener raised: %s", exc)
   173→

   175→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   176→# Plugin System                                                              #
   177→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   178→class Plugin(Protocol):
   179→    """
   180→    Public interface that all plugins must comply with.
   181→    """
   182→
   183→    name: str
   184→    version: str
   185→
   186→    def setup(self, bus: EventBus) -> None: ...
   187→    async def start(self) -> None: ...
   188→    async def shutdown(self) -> None: ...
   189→
   190→
   191→class PluginLoader:
   192→    """
   193→    Discover & instantiate plugins.
   194→
   195→    *User* plugins are loaded from the following search order:
   196→
   197→    1. `$FLOCKDESK_PLUGINS` (semicolon separated list of paths)
   198→    2. `~/.flockdesk/plugins`
   199→    3. `<install-root>/flockdesk/plugins`
   200→    """
   201→
   202→    SEARCH_LOCATIONS: Tuple[Path, ...] = (
   203→        Path(os.getenv("FLOCKDESK_PLUGINS", "")),
   204→        Path.home() / ".flockdesk" / "plugins",
   205→        Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "plugins",
   206→    )
   207→
   208→    def __init__(self, bus: EventBus) -> None:
   209→        self._bus = bus
   210→        self._plugins: List[Plugin] = []
   211→
   212→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   213→    # Discovery                                                             #
   214→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   215→    def discover(self) -> List[Plugin]:
   216→        logger.info("Discovering plugins …")
   217→        for location in self.SEARCH_LOCATIONS:
   218→            if not location or not location.exists():
   219→                continue
   220→            for path in location.glob("*/__init__.py"):
   221→                try:
   222→                    plugin = self._load_module(path)
   223→                    self._plugins.append(plugin)
   224→                    logger.info("Loaded plugin: %s %s", plugin.name, plugin.version)
   225→                except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except
   226→                    logger.error(
   227→                        "Failed to load plugin at %s:\n%s",
   228→                        path,
   229→                        traceback.format_exc(),
   230→                    )
   231→        return self._plugins
   232→
   233→    def _load_module(self, init_file: Path) -> Plugin:
   234→        module_name = f"flockdesk.plugin.{init_file.parent.name}"
   235→        spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(module_name, init_file)
   236→        if not spec or not spec.loader:
   237→            raise ImportError(f"Cannot build spec for {init_file}")
   238→        module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
   239→        sys.modules[module_name] = module
   240→        spec.loader.exec_module(module)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
   241→
   242→        if not hasattr(module, "Plugin"):
   243→            raise AttributeError(
   244→                f"{module_name} does not expose a 'Plugin' class"
   245→            )  # noqa: TRY003
   246→
   247→        plugin_cls = getattr(module, "Plugin")
   248→        plugin: Plugin = plugin_cls()  # type: ignore[call-arg]
   249→
   250→        plugin.setup(self._bus)
   251→        return plugin
   252→
   253→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   254→    # Lifecycle                                                             #
   255→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   256→    async def start_all(self) -> None:
   257→        for plugin in self._plugins:
   258→            try:
   259→                await plugin.start()
   260→                logger.info("Plugin started: %s", plugin.name)
   261→            except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except
   262→                logger.exception("Plugin %s failed to start", plugin.name)
   263→
   264→    async def shutdown_all(self) -> None:
   265→        for plugin in reversed(self._plugins):
   266→            try:
   267→                await plugin.shutdown()
   268→                logger.info("Plugin stopped: %s", plugin.name)
   269→            except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except
   270→                logger.exception("Plugin %s failed to shutdown", plugin.name)
   271→

   273→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   274→# Settings / Configuration                                                   #
   275→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   276→class SettingsManager:
   277→    """
   278→    Persisted key/value store backed by a JSON blob in the user's config dir.
   279→    """
   280→
   281→    FILE_NAME = "settings.json"
   282→
   283→    def __init__(self) -> None:
   284→        self._data: Dict[str, JSON_T] = {}
   285→        self._path = self._resolve_path()
   286→        self._load()
   287→
   288→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   289→    # Public API                                                            #
   290→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   291→    def get(self, key: str, default: JSON_T | None = None) -> JSON_T | None:
   292→        return self._data.get(key, default)
   293→
   294→    def set(self, key: str, value: JSON_T) -> None:
   295→        self._data[key] = value
   296→        self._save_async()
   297→
   298→    def all(self) -> Dict[str, JSON_T]:
   299→        return dict(self._data)
   300→
   301→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   302→    # Persistence                                                           #
   303→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   304→    def _load(self) -> None:
   305→        try:
   306→            if self._path.exists():
   307→                self._data = json.loads(self._path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   308→                logger.debug("Settings loaded from %s", self._path)
   309→        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError) as exc:
   310→            logger.warning("Failed to load settings (%s), starting fresh", exc)
   311→            self._data = {}
   312→
   313→    def _save_async(self) -> None:
   314→        """
   315→        Off-load writing to disk to avoid blocking the GUI / event-loop.
   316→        """
   317→
   318→        async def _writer() -> None:
   319→            try:
   320→                self._path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   321→                self._path.write_text(json.dumps(self._data, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
   322→                logger.debug("Settings persisted to %s", self._path)
   323→            except OSError as exc:
   324→                logger.error("Could not persist settings: %s", exc)
   325→
   326→        asyncio.get_event_loop().create_task(_writer())
   327→
   328→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   329→    # Helpers                                                               #
   330→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   331→    @staticmethod
   332→    def _resolve_path() -> Path:
   333→        if appdirs:
   334→            cfg_dir = Path(appdirs.user_config_dir("FlockDesk", "Flockware"))
   335→        else:
   336→            cfg_dir = Path.home() / ".config" / "flockdesk"
   337→        return cfg_dir / SettingsManager.FILE_NAME
   338→
   339→
   340→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   341→# Crash / Diagnostics                                                        #
   342→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   343→class CrashReporter:
   344→    """
   345→    Thin wrapper around Sentry so we can easily replace/disable later on.
   346→    """
   347→
   348→    def __init__(self, dsn: Optional[str] = None) -> None:
   349→        self._enabled: bool = False
   350→        if dsn and sentry_sdk:
   351→            sentry_sdk.init(dsn=dsn, release="flockdesk@1.0.0")
   352→            self._enabled = True
   353→            logger.info("Crash reporting enabled")
   354→        elif dsn:
   355→            logger.warning("sentry-sdk not installed, crash reporting disabled")
   356→
   357→    async def capture_exception(self, exc: BaseException) -> None:
   358→        if self._enabled:
   359→            sentry_sdk.capture_exception(exc)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
   360→
   361→
   362→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   363→# Core Application                                                           #
   364→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   365→class FlockDeskCoreApp(QObject if QObject is not object else object):  # type: ignore[misc]
   366→    """
   367→    Central bootstrap for FlockDesk.
   368→
   369→    Spins up the event-bus, plugin runtime, setting manager, and bridges
   370→    between the asyncio reactor and Qt's message-pump if available.
   371→    """
   372→
   373→    def __init__(self, *, headless: bool = False) -> None:
   374→        if not headless and QCoreApplication is None:
   375→            raise RuntimeError(
   376→                "PySide6 not installed ‑ cannot run in GUI mode. "
   377→                "Pass `headless=True` to run without Qt."
   378→            )
   379→
   380→        super().__init__()  # type: ignore[misc]
   381→        self._headless = headless
   382→        self._loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()
   383→        asyncio.set_event_loop(self._loop)
   384→
   385→        self.bus = EventBus()
   386→        self.settings = SettingsManager()
   387→        self.crash = CrashReporter(
   388→            dsn=self.settings.get("sentry_dsn")  # type: ignore[arg-type]
   389→        )
   390→        self._plugin_loader = PluginLoader(self.bus)
   391→
   392→        self._closing = asyncio.Event()
   393→
   394→        # Connect SIGINT / SIGTERM
   395→        for sig in (signal.SIGINT, signal.SIGTERM):
   396→            signal.signal(sig, self._signal_handler)
   397→
   398→        if not self._headless:
   399→            self._qt_app: QCoreApplication = QCoreApplication(sys.argv)  # type: ignore[assignment]
   400→            # Pump asyncio via Qt's event loop every 15ms
   401→            QTimer.singleShot(0, self._qt_asyncio_integration_tick)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
   402→
   403→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   404→    # Bootstrap                                                             #
   405→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   406→    def run(self) -> None:
   407→        """
   408→        Synchronous entry-point; block until shutdown completes.
   409→        """
   410→        logger.info("Starting FlockDesk …")
   411→        try:
   412→            self._loop.run_until_complete(self._async_main())
   413→        finally:
   414→            self._loop.run_until_complete(self._loop.shutdown_asyncgens())
   415→            self._loop.close()
   416→            logger.info("FlockDesk stopped")
   417→
   418→    async def _async_main(self) -> None:
   419→        """
   420→        Coroutine that manages the asynchronous lifecycle.
   421→        """
   422→        # Plugin discovery/startup
   423→        self._plugin_loader.discover()
   424→        await self._plugin_loader.start_all()
   425→
   426→        # Run until a shutdown signal is received
   427→        await self._closing.wait()
   428→
   429→        # Tear-down
   430→        await self._plugin_loader.shutdown_all()
   431→
   432→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   433→    # Shutdown & Signals                                                    #
   434→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   435→    def _signal_handler(self, signum: int, _frame: Any | None = None) -> None:
   436→        logger.info("Received signal %s, shutting down …", signum)
   437→        self._loop.call_soon_threadsafe(self._closing.set)
   438→
   439→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   440→    # Qt / Async Integration                                                #
   441→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   442→    def _qt_asyncio_integration_tick(self) -> None:  # pragma: no cover
   443→        """
   444→        Execute all asyncio tasks waiting on the loop & re-schedule next tick.
   445→        """
   446→        try:
   447→            self._loop.call_soon(self._loop.stop)
   448→            self._loop.run_forever()
   449→        except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except
   450→            logger.exception("Error during asyncio-Qt integration tick: %s", exc)
   451→            self._loop.create_task(self.crash.capture_exception(exc))
   452→        finally:
   453→            # Re-schedule as long as the application is alive
   454→            if not self._closing.is_set():
   455→                QTimer.singleShot(15, self._qt_asyncio_integration_tick)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
   456→
   457→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   458→    # Convenience helpers                                                   #
   459→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   460→    def quit(self) -> None:
   461→        """
   462→        Can be called by plugins/UI to request a graceful shutdown.
   463→        """
   464→        self._closing.set()
   465→
   466→
   467→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   468→# Entrypoint                                                                 #
   469→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   470→def _main() -> None:  # pragma: no cover
   471→    """
   472→    CLI bootstrap mainly for development/testing purposes.
   473→    """
   474→    import argparse
   475→
   476→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Launch FlockDesk core service")
   477→    parser.add_argument(
   478→        "--headless",
   479→        action="store_true",
   480→        help="Run without Qt (no GUI) – suitable for CI or server mode.",
   481→    )
   482→    args = parser.parse_args()
   483→
   484→    app = FlockDeskCoreApp(headless=args.headless)
   485→    app.run()
   486→
   487→
   488→if __name__ == "__main__":
   489→    _main()

--------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Discovery                                                             #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def discover(self) -> List[Plugin]:\n        logger.info(\"Discovering plugins …\")\n        for location in self.SEARCH_LOCATIONS:\n            if not location or not location.exists():\n                continue\n            for path in location.glob(\"*/__init__.py\"):\n                try:\n                    plugin = self._load_module(path)\n                    self._plugins.append(plugin)\n                    logger.info(\"Loaded plugin: %s %s\", plugin.name, plugin.version)\n                except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n                    logger.error(\n                        \"Failed to load plugin at %s:\\n%s\",\n                        path,\n                        traceback.format_exc(),\n                    )\n        return self._plugins\n\n    def _load_module(self, init_file: Path) -> Plugin:\n        module_name = f\"flockdesk.plugin.{init_file.parent.name}\"\n        spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(module_name, init_file)\n        if not spec or not spec.loader:\n            raise ImportError(f\"Cannot build spec for {init_file}\")\n        module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)\n        sys.modules[module_name] = module\n        spec.loader.exec_module(module)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n\n        if not hasattr(module, \"Plugin\"):\n            raise AttributeError(\n                f\"{module_name} does not expose a 'Plugin' class\"\n            )  # noqa: TRY003\n\n        plugin_cls = getattr(module, \"Plugin\")\n        plugin: Plugin = plugin_cls()  # type: ignore[call-arg]\n\n        plugin.setup(self._bus)\n        return plugin\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Lifecycle                                                             #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    async def start_all(self) -> None:\n        for plugin in self._plugins:\n            try:\n                await plugin.start()\n                logger.info(\"Plugin started: %s\", plugin.name)\n            except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n                logger.exception(\"Plugin %s failed to start\", plugin.name)\n\n    async def shutdown_all(self) -> None:\n        for plugin in reversed(self._plugins):\n            try:\n                await plugin.shutdown()\n                logger.info(\"Plugin stopped: %s\", plugin.name)\n            except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n                logger.exception(\"Plugin %s failed to shutdown\", plugin.name)\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Settings / Configuration                                                   #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nclass SettingsManager:\n    \"\"\"\n    Persisted key/value store backed by a JSON blob in the user's config dir.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    FILE_NAME = \"settings.json\"\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self._data: Dict[str, JSON_T] = {}\n        self._path = self._resolve_path()\n        self._load()\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Public API                                                            #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def get(self, key: str, default: JSON_T | None = None) -> JSON_T | None:\n        return self._data.get(key, default)\n\n    def set(self, key: str, value: JSON_T) -> None:\n        self._data[key] = value\n        self._save_async()\n\n    def all(self) -> Dict[str, JSON_T]:\n        return dict(self._data)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Persistence                                                           #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def _load(self) -> None:\n        try:\n            if self._path.exists():\n                self._data = json.loads(self._path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n                logger.debug(\"Settings loaded from %s\", self._path)\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError) as exc:\n            logger.warning(\"Failed to load settings (%s), starting fresh\", exc)\n            self._data = {}\n\n    def _save_async(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Off-load writing to disk to avoid blocking the GUI / event-loop.\n        \"\"\"\n\n        async def _writer() -> None:\n            try:\n                self._path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n                self._path.write_text(json.dumps(self._data, indent=2), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n                logger.debug(\"Settings persisted to %s\", self._path)\n            except OSError as exc:\n                logger.error(\"Could not persist settings: %s\", exc)\n\n        asyncio.get_event_loop().create_task(_writer())\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Helpers                                                               #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    @staticmethod\n    def _resolve_path() -> Path:\n        if appdirs:\n            cfg_dir = Path(appdirs.user_config_dir(\"FlockDesk\", \"Flockware\"))\n        else:\n            cfg_dir = Path.home() / \".config\" / \"flockdesk\"\n        return cfg_dir / SettingsManager.FILE_NAME\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Crash / Diagnostics                                                        #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nclass CrashReporter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Thin wrapper around Sentry so we can easily replace/disable later on.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, dsn: Optional[str] = None) -> None:\n        self._enabled: bool = False\n        if dsn and sentry_sdk:\n            sentry_sdk.init(dsn=dsn, release=\"flockdesk@1.0.0\")\n            self._enabled = True\n            logger.info(\"Crash reporting enabled\")\n        elif dsn:\n            logger.warning(\"sentry-sdk not installed, crash reporting disabled\")\n\n    async def capture_exception(self, exc: BaseException) -> None:\n        if self._enabled:\n            sentry_sdk.capture_exception(exc)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Core Application                                                           #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nclass FlockDeskCoreApp(QObject if QObject is not object else object):  # type: ignore[misc]\n    \"\"\"\n    Central bootstrap for FlockDesk.\n\n    Spins up the event-bus, plugin runtime, setting manager, and bridges\n    between the asyncio reactor and Qt's message-pump if available.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, *, headless: bool = False) -> None:\n        if not headless and QCoreApplication is None:\n            raise RuntimeError(\n                \"PySide6 not installed ‑ cannot run in GUI mode. \"\n                \"Pass `headless=True` to run without Qt.\"\n            )\n\n        super().__init__()  # type: ignore[misc]\n        self._headless = headless\n        self._loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()\n        asyncio.set_event_loop(self._loop)\n\n        self.bus = EventBus()\n        self.settings = SettingsManager()\n        self.crash = CrashReporter(\n            dsn=self.settings.get(\"sentry_dsn\")  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n        )\n        self._plugin_loader = PluginLoader(self.bus)\n\n        self._closing = asyncio.Event()\n\n        # Connect SIGINT / SIGTERM\n        for sig in (signal.SIGINT, signal.SIGTERM):\n            signal.signal(sig, self._signal_handler)\n\n        if not self._headless:\n            self._qt_app: QCoreApplication = QCoreApplication(sys.argv)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n            # Pump asyncio via Qt's event loop every 15ms\n            QTimer.singleShot(0, self._qt_asyncio_integration_tick)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Bootstrap                                                             #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def run(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Synchronous entry-point; block until shutdown completes.\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.info(\"Starting FlockDesk …\")\n        try:\n            self._loop.run_until_complete(self._async_main())\n        finally:\n            self._loop.run_until_complete(self._loop.shutdown_asyncgens())\n            self._loop.close()\n            logger.info(\"FlockDesk stopped\")\n\n    async def _async_main(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Coroutine that manages the asynchronous lifecycle.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Plugin discovery/startup\n        self._plugin_loader.discover()\n        await self._plugin_loader.start_all()\n\n        # Run until a shutdown signal is received\n        await self._closing.wait()\n\n        # Tear-down\n        await self._plugin_loader.shutdown_all()\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Shutdown & Signals                                                    #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def _signal_handler(self, signum: int, _frame: Any | None = None) -> None:\n        logger.info(\"Received signal %s, shutting down …\", signum)\n        self._loop.call_soon_threadsafe(self._closing.set)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Qt / Async Integration                                                #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def _qt_asyncio_integration_tick(self) -> None:  # pragma: no cover\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute all asyncio tasks waiting on the loop & re-schedule next tick.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            self._loop.call_soon(self._loop.stop)\n            self._loop.run_forever()\n        except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n            logger.exception(\"Error during asyncio-Qt integration tick: %s\", exc)\n            self._loop.create_task(self.crash.capture_exception(exc))\n        finally:\n            # Re-schedule as long as the application is alive\n            if not self._closing.is_set():\n                QTimer.singleShot(15, self._qt_asyncio_integration_tick)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Convenience helpers                                                   #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def quit(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Can be called by plugins/UI to request a graceful shutdown.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._closing.set()\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Entrypoint                                                                 #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\ndef _main() -> None:  # pragma: no cover\n    \"\"\"\n    CLI bootstrap mainly for development/testing purposes.\n    \"\"\"\n    import argparse\n\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"Launch FlockDesk core service\")\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--headless\",\n        action=\"store_true\",\n        help=\"Run without Qt (no GUI) – suitable for CI or server mode.\",\n    )\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    app = FlockDeskCoreApp(headless=args.headless)\n    app.run()\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    _main()\n```", "numLines": 490, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 490}}

     2→"""
     3→FlockDesk – Chat Module
     4→View-Model (MVVM) for the Chat micro-front-end.
     5→
     6→This module owns the presentation-ready state of a single chat channel.
     7→It keeps the Qt View in sync with the domain layer through an internal
     8→event-bus, handles optimistic UI updates, provides command objects for
     9→the Controller, and persists transient UI data such as unsent drafts.
    10→
    11→Author: FlockDesk Core Team
    12→"""
    13→
    14→from __future__ import annotations
    15→
    16→import json
    17→import logging
    18→import threading
    19→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    20→from datetime import datetime, timezone
    21→from pathlib import Path
    22→from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
    23→
    24→from PySide6 import QtCore
    25→
    26→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#
    27→# External / Project-wide Dependencies
    28→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#
    29→try:
    30→    from flockdesk.core.events import EventBus, Event  # type: ignore
    31→    from flockdesk.core.settings import settings  # type: ignore
    32→    from flockdesk.core.exceptions import ChatSendError  # type: ignore
    33→except ModuleNotFoundError:  # Fallbacks for static type checkers / docs
    34→    EventBus = Any  # pragma: no cover
    35→    Event = Any  # pragma: no cover
    36→    settings = {}  # pragma: no cover
    37→    ChatSendError = Exception  # pragma: no cover
    38→
    39→
    40→_log = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    41→
    42→
    43→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#
    44→# Data Models
    45→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#
    46→@dataclass(slots=True, frozen=True)
    47→class ChatMessage:
    48→    """Immutably represents a message entity in the chat session."""
    49→
    50→    msg_id: str
    51→    channel_id: str
    52→    sender_id: str
    53→    sender_display: str
    54→    content: str
    55→    timestamp: datetime
    56→    edited: bool = False
    57→    is_outgoing: bool = False
    58→    attachments: List[Path] = field(default_factory=list)
    59→
    60→    def serialize(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    61→        return {
    62→            "msg_id": self.msg_id,
    63→            "channel_id": self.channel_id,
    64→            "sender_id": self.sender_id,
    65→            "sender_display": self.sender_display,
    66→            "content": self.content,
    67→            "timestamp": self.timestamp.isoformat(),
    68→            "edited": self.edited,
    69→            "is_outgoing": self.is_outgoing,
    70→            "attachments": [str(p) for p in self.attachments],
    71→        }
    72→
    73→
    74→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#
    75→# Chat View-Model
    76→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#
    77→class ChatViewModel(QtCore.QObject):
    78→    """
    79→    Acts as the View-Model for a single chat channel.
    80→
    81→    Responsibilities
    82→    ----------------
    83→    • Keep a local in-memory list of ChatMessage objects
    84→    • Subscribe to the domain event-bus for incoming/outgoing messages
    85→    • Expose Qt signals so Views can bind via Qt’s signal/slot system
    86→    • Persist unsent drafts & restore them on reboot
    87→    """
    88→
    89→    # -------- Qt Signals ----------------------------------------------------#
    90→    messageAdded = QtCore.Signal(ChatMessage)  # new message appended
    91→    messageReplaced = QtCore.Signal(ChatMessage)  # edited or updated
    92→    typingStatusChanged = QtCore.Signal(str, bool)  # user_id, is_typing
    93→    errorOccurred = QtCore.Signal(str)  # human-readable error string
    94→    historyLoaded = QtCore.Signal(list)  # list[ChatMessage]
    95→
    96→    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#
    97→    def __init__(self, channel_id: str, event_bus: EventBus, parent=None) -> None:
    98→        super().__init__(parent)
    99→        self._channel_id: str = channel_id
   100→        self._event_bus: EventBus = event_bus
   101→        self._messages: list[ChatMessage] = []
   102→
   103→        # drafts/<user>/<channel>.json
   104→        self._draft_path: Path = (
   105→            Path(settings.get("user_data_dir", "~/.flockdesk")).expanduser()
   106→            / "drafts"
   107→            / f"{settings.get('user_id', 'anonymous')}"
   108→            / f"{channel_id}.json"
   109→        )
   110→
   111→        self._typing_users: Dict[str, QtCore.QTimer] = {}
   112→        self._lock = threading.RLock()
   113→
   114→        self._register_event_subscriptions()
   115→        self._restore_draft_async()
   116→
   117→    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#
   118→    # API surface for Controllers
   119→    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#
   120→    def history(self) -> list[ChatMessage]:
   121→        """Return a copy of the current message history."""
   122→        with self._lock:
   123→            return list(self._messages)
   124→
   125→    def send_message(self, content: str, attachments: Optional[List[Path]] = None) -> None:
   126→        """
   127→        Send a message to the current channel.
   128→
   129→        The actual network interaction is delegated to the domain layer
   130→        via the event-bus. We optimistically append the message locally
   131→        and mark it as `is_outgoing=True`; once the server acknowledges
   132→        (or rejects) the message we will replace/update it.
   133→        """
   134→        if not content and not attachments:
   135→            _log.debug("Empty message suppressed")
   136→            return
   137→
   138→        msg_id = f"tmp-{datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc).timestamp()}"
   139→        attachments = attachments or []
   140→        outgoing = ChatMessage(
   141→            msg_id=msg_id,
   142→            channel_id=self._channel_id,
   143→            sender_id=settings.get("user_id", "anonymous"),
   144→            sender_display=settings.get("user_display", "Me"),
   145→            content=content,
   146→            timestamp=datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc),
   147→            is_outgoing=True,
   148→            attachments=attachments,
   149→        )
   150→
   151→        self._append_message(outgoing)
   152→
   153→        # Publish to domain event-bus (fire-and-forget)
   154→        _log.debug("Publishing OutgoingMessage event: %s", outgoing.msg_id)
   155→        self._event_bus.publish(
   156→            Event(
   157→                topic="chat.outgoing",
   158→                payload=outgoing.serialize(),
   159→                replayable=False,
   160→            )
   161→        )
   162→
   163→        # Clear persisted draft for this channel
   164→        self._clear_draft_file()
   165→
   166→    def start_typing(self) -> None:
   167→        """Publish typing indicator event when user starts typing."""
   168→        self._event_bus.publish(
   169→            Event(
   170→                topic="chat.typing",
   171→                payload={"channel_id": self._channel_id, "user_id": settings.get("user_id")},
   172→            )
   173→        )
   174→
   175→    def persist_draft(self, draft_text: str) -> None:
   176→        """
   177→        Persist unsent draft on disk.
   178→
   179→        Called from the Controller on text-changed events.
   180→        """
   181→        if not draft_text.strip():
   182→            self._clear_draft_file()
   183→            return
   184→
   185→        self._draft_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   186→        try:
   187→            self._draft_path.write_text(
   188→                json.dumps({"draft": draft_text, "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat()}),
   189→                encoding="utf-8",
   190→            )
   191→            _log.debug("Draft persisted to %s", self._draft_path)
   192→        except OSError as exc:
   193→            _log.warning("Failed to write draft file: %s", exc)
   194→
   195→    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#
   196→    # Internal helpers
   197→    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#
   198→    def _register_event_subscriptions(self) -> None:
   199→        """
   200→        Subscribe to the global EventBus for chat-related events.
   201→
   202→        We keep the subscription tokens so we can unsubscribe
   203→        when the View-Model is deleted / the window is closed.
   204→        """
   205→
   206→        # Incoming messages
   207→        self._event_bus.subscribe(
   208→            "chat.incoming",
   209→            self._on_incoming_message,
   210→            group=f"vm_{self._channel_id}",
   211→        )
   212→
   213→        # Delivery acknowledgements for optimistic updates
   214→        self._event_bus.subscribe(
   215→            "chat.outgoing.confirmation",
   216→            self._on_delivery_confirmation,
   217→            group=f"vm_{self._channel_id}",
   218→        )
   219→
   220→        # Typing indicators
   221→        self._event_bus.subscribe(
   222→            "chat.typing",
   223→            self._on_typing_indicator,
   224→            group=f"vm_{self._channel_id}",
   225→        )
   226→
   227→        # Synchronised edits / deletions
   228→        self._event_bus.subscribe(
   229→            "chat.message.updated",
   230→            self._on_remote_update,
   231→            group=f"vm_{self._channel_id}",
   232→        )
   233→
   234→    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#
   235→    def _append_message(self, message: ChatMessage) -> None:
   236→        with self._lock:
   237→            self._messages.append(message)
   238→        self.messageAdded.emit(message)
   239→
   240→    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#
   241→    # Event-bus callback handlers
   242→    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#
   243→    def _on_incoming_message(self, event: Event) -> None:
   244→        """Handle new message events from other users."""
   245→        payload = event.payload
   246→        if payload["channel_id"] != self._channel_id:
   247→            return
   248→
   249→        msg = ChatMessage(
   250→            msg_id=payload["msg_id"],
   251→            channel_id=payload["channel_id"],
   252→            sender_id=payload["sender_id"],
   253→            sender_display=payload["sender_display"],
   254→            content=payload["content"],
   255→            timestamp=datetime.fromisoformat(payload["timestamp"]),
   256→            attachments=[Path(p) for p in payload.get("attachments", [])],
   257→        )
   258→        self._append_message(msg)
   259→
   260→    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#
   261→    def _on_delivery_confirmation(self, event: Event) -> None:
   262→        """Replace optimistic message with definitive server copy."""
   263→        payload = event.payload
   264→        if payload["channel_id"] != self._channel_id:
   265→            return
   266→
   267→        tmp_id = payload["tmp_id"]
   268→        real_id = payload["msg_id"]
   269→
   270→        with self._lock:
   271→            for idx, m in enumerate(self._messages):
   272→                if m.msg_id == tmp_id:
   273→                    updated = ChatMessage(
   274→                        msg_id=real_id,
   275→                        channel_id=m.channel_id,
   276→                        sender_id=m.sender_id,
   277→                        sender_display=m.sender_display,
   278→                        content=m.content,
   279→                        timestamp=datetime.fromisoformat(payload["timestamp"]),
   280→                        is_outgoing=False,
   281→                        attachments=m.attachments,
   282→                    )
   283→                    self._messages[idx] = updated
   284→                    _log.debug("Optimistic message %s replaced by %s", tmp_id, real_id)
   285→                    self.messageReplaced.emit(updated)
   286→                    break
   287→
   288→    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#
   289→    def _on_typing_indicator(self, event: Event) -> None:
   290→        """Update typing indicator map & emit signal."""
   291→        payload = event.payload
   292→        if payload["channel_id"] != self._channel_id:
   293→            return
   294→
   295→        user_id = payload["user_id"]
   296→        if user_id == settings.get("user_id"):
   297→            return  # ignore self typing
   298→
   299→        # Reset/Start debounce timer for this user
   300→        timer = self._typing_users.get(user_id)
   301→        if timer is None:
   302→            timer = QtCore.QTimer(self)
   303→            timer.setSingleShot(True)
   304→            timer.timeout.connect(lambda uid=user_id: self._emit_typing_end(uid))
   305→            self._typing_users[user_id] = timer
   306→            self.typingStatusChanged.emit(user_id, True)
   307→
   308→        timer.start(3000)  # 3s window
   309→
   310→    def _emit_typing_end(self, user_id: str) -> None:
   311→        self.typingStatusChanged.emit(user_id, False)
   312→        self._typing_users.pop(user_id, None)
   313→
   314→    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#
   315→    def _on_remote_update(self, event: Event) -> None:
   316→        """Handle remote edits or deletions."""
   317→        payload = event.payload
   318→        if payload["channel_id"] != self._channel_id:
   319→            return
   320→
   321→        with self._lock:
   322→            for idx, m in enumerate(self._messages):
   323→                if m.msg_id == payload["msg_id"]:
   324→                    updated = ChatMessage(
   325→                        msg_id=m.msg_id,
   326→                        channel_id=m.channel_id,
   327→                        sender_id=m.sender_id,
   328→                        sender_display=m.sender_display,
   329→                        content=payload.get("content", m.content),
   330→                        timestamp=m.timestamp,
   331→                        edited=True,
   332→                        attachments=[Path(p) for p in payload.get("attachments", [])],
   333→                    )
   334→                    self._messages[idx] = updated
   335→                    self.messageReplaced.emit(updated)
   336→                    _log.debug("Message %s updated from remote", m.msg_id)
   337→                    break
   338→
   339→    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#
   340→    # Draft persistence
   341→    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#
   342→    def _restore_draft_async(self) -> None:
   343→        """Restore draft in a background thread to keep UI responsive."""
   344→
   345→        def _worker(vm: "ChatViewModel") -> None:
   346→            if vm._draft_path.exists():
   347→                try:
   348→                    data = json.loads(vm._draft_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   349→                    draft = data.get("draft", "")
   350→                    _log.debug("Draft restored for channel %s", vm._channel_id)
   351→                    # Emit via Qt to avoid cross-thread issues
   352→                    QtCore.QMetaObject.invokeMethod(
   353→                        vm,
   354→                        "_emit_draft_restored",

   356→                        QtCore.Q_ARG(str, draft),
   357→                    )

   361→        threading.Thread(target=_worker, args=(self,), daemon=True).start()
   362→
   363→    @QtCore.Slot(str)
   364→    def _emit_draft_restored(self, text: str) -> None:
   365→        self.historyLoaded.emit([])  # just to notify view to attach draft text
   366→
   367→    def _clear_draft_file(self) -> None:

   370→                self._draft_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)
   371→                _log.debug("Draft cleared for channel %s", self._channel_id)
   372→        except OSError as exc:
   373→            _log.warning("Failed to clear draft file: %s", exc)
   374→
   375→    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#
   376→    # Qt Object Life-Cycle
   377→    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#
   378→    def __del__(self) -> None:
   379→        """Ensure we cleanly unsubscribe from the EventBus."""
   380→        try:
   381→            self._event_bus.unsubscribe_group(f"vm_{self._channel_id}")
   382→        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover
   383→            pass  # During interpreter shutdown objects may be gone
   384→
   385→
   386→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#
   387→# Factory (Dependency Injection ready)
   388→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#
   389→def create_chat_viewmodel(channel_id: str, event_bus: EventBus) -> ChatViewModel:
   390→    """
   391→    Factory helper so the app’s DI container can easily inject dependencies.
   392→
   393→    Returns
   394→    -------
   395→    ChatViewModel
   396→        Ready-to-use instance with subscriptions in place.
   397→    """
   398→    vm = ChatViewModel(channel_id=channel_id, event_bus=event_bus)
   399→    return vm

-----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    def _append_message(self, message: ChatMessage) -> None:\n        with self._lock:\n            self._messages.append(message)\n        self.messageAdded.emit(message)\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Event-bus callback handlers\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    def _on_incoming_message(self, event: Event) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Handle new message events from other users.\"\"\"\n        payload = event.payload\n        if payload[\"channel_id\"] != self._channel_id:\n            return\n\n        msg = ChatMessage(\n            msg_id=payload[\"msg_id\"],\n            channel_id=payload[\"channel_id\"],\n            sender_id=payload[\"sender_id\"],\n            sender_display=payload[\"sender_display\"],\n            content=payload[\"content\"],\n            timestamp=datetime.fromisoformat(payload[\"timestamp\"]),\n            attachments=[Path(p) for p in payload.get(\"attachments\", [])],\n        )\n        self._append_message(msg)\n\n    #
