   141→class ProfileService:
   142→    """High-level facade around local/remote profile management."""
   143→
   144→    # pylint: disable=too-many-instance-attributes
   145→    def __init__(
   146→        self,
   147→        event_bus: EventBus,
   148→        user_id: str,
   149→        *,
   150→        storage: Optional[StorageProvider] = None,
   151→        remote_endpoint: str = DEFAULT_CLOUD_ENDPOINT,
   152→        sync_interval_s: int = 120,
   153→        max_backoff_s: int = 10 * 60,
   154→        session: Optional[requests.Session] = None,
   155→    ) -> None:
   156→        self._bus = event_bus
   157→        self._user_id = user_id
   158→        self._storage = storage or StorageProvider()
   159→        self._remote_endpoint = remote_endpoint.rstrip("/")
   160→        self._sync_interval = max(15, sync_interval_s)  # sane lower bound
   161→        self._max_backoff = max_backoff_s
   162→        self._session = session or requests.Session()
   163→        self._lock = threading.RLock()
   164→
   165→        self._listeners: List[Callable[[UserProfile], None]] = []
   166→        self._profile: UserProfile = (
   167→            self._storage.load(user_id) or self._initialise_blank_profile()
   168→        )
   169→
   170→        self._sync_thread = threading.Thread(
   171→            target=self._sync_loop,
   172→            name="FlockDeskProfileSync",
   173→            daemon=True,
   174→        )
   175→        self._stop_event = threading.Event()
   176→
   177→        # subscribe to in-process events
   178→        self._bus.subscribe("profile/refresh", self._on_external_refresh)
   179→
   180→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   181→    # lifecycle
   182→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   183→
   184→    def start(self) -> None:
   185→        """Start the background sync loop."""
   186→        if not self._sync_thread.is_alive():
   187→            LOG.debug("Starting ProfileService sync thread for user %s", self._user_id)
   188→            self._sync_thread.start()
   189→
   190→    def stop(self) -> None:
   191→        """Signal the background thread to terminate and wait for completion."""
   192→        LOG.debug("Stopping ProfileService sync thread for user %s", self._user_id)
   193→        self._stop_event.set()
   194→        self._sync_thread.join(timeout=5)
   195→
   196→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   197→    # public – profile CRUD
   198→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   199→
   200→    @property
   201→    def profile(self) -> UserProfile:
   202→        with self._lock:
   203→            return self._profile
   204→
   205→    def save(self, profile: UserProfile, *, broadcast: bool = True) -> None:
   206→        """Persist and broadcast a profile that has been modified in-memory."""
   207→        with self._lock:
   208→            profile.last_updated_ts = time.time()
   209→            self._profile = profile
   210→            self._storage.save(profile)
   211→
   212→        if broadcast:
   213→            self._bus.publish(Event("profile/updated", payload=profile.to_json()))
   214→            self._notify_listeners(profile)
   215→
   216→    def update_preferences(self, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
   217→        """Convenience helper to modify `preferences`."""
   218→        with self._lock:
   219→            self._profile.preferences.update(kwargs)
   220→            self._profile.last_updated_ts = time.time()
   221→            self._storage.save(self._profile)
   222→
   223→        self._bus.publish(Event("profile/updated", payload=self._profile.to_json()))
   224→        self._notify_listeners(self._profile)
   225→
   226→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
   227→    # observer API
   228→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
   229→
   230→    def register_listener(self, fn: Callable[[UserProfile], None]) -> Callable[[], None]:
   231→        """Register a callback that fires when the profile changes.
   232→
   233→        Returns
   234→        -------
   235→        Callable[[], None]
   236→            Unregister function.
   237→        """
   238→        if fn not in self._listeners:
   239→            self._listeners.append(fn)
   240→
   241→        def _unregister() -> None:
   242→            try:
   243→                self._listeners.remove(fn)
   244→            except ValueError:
   245→                pass
   246→
   247→        return _unregister
   248→
   249→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
   250→    # private – event bus hooks
   251→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
   252→
   253→    def _on_external_refresh(self, event: Event) -> None:  # noqa: D401
   254→        """Handle 'profile/refresh' emitted by external components."""
   255→        LOG.debug("Received external profile refresh request event=%s", event.meta)
   256→        # Force an immediate remote sync outside of the normal interval
   257→        threading.Thread(
   258→            target=self._pull_remote, name="FlockDeskProfileForcePull", daemon=True
   259→        ).start()
   260→
   261→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
   262→    # private – synchronisation loop
   263→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
   264→
   265→    def _sync_loop(self) -> None:
   266→        """Background loop – push/pull profile with exponential backoff."""
   267→        backoff_s = self._sync_interval
   268→        while not self._stop_event.is_set():
   269→            start = time.time()
   270→            try:
   271→                self._push_local()    # push first so remote gets our changes
   272→                self._pull_remote()   # then pull remote updates
   273→                backoff_s = self._sync_interval  # reset backoff after success
   274→            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 – catch-all keeps the loop alive
   275→                LOG.exception("Profile sync failed – backing off")
   276→                backoff_s = min(backoff_s * 2, self._max_backoff)
   277→            finally:
   278→                elapsed = time.time() - start
   279→                sleep_time = max(backoff_s - elapsed, 1)
   280→                self._stop_event.wait(timeout=sleep_time)
   281→
   282→    def _push_local(self) -> None:
   283→        """Push local profile to the cloud if it has diverged."""
   284→        with self._lock:
   285→            payload = self._profile.to_json()
   286→
   287→        url = f"{self._remote_endpoint}/{self._user_id}"
   288→        try:
   289→            res = self._session.put(url, json=payload, timeout=10)
   290→            res.raise_for_status()
   291→            LOG.debug("Pushed profile to remote (%s)", res.status_code)
   292→        except requests.RequestException:
   293→            LOG.warning("Failed to push profile to cloud", exc_info=True)
   294→            raise
   295→
   296→    def _pull_remote(self) -> None:
   297→        """Pull profile from cloud and merge if newer than local."""
   298→        url = f"{self._remote_endpoint}/{self._user_id}"
   299→        try:
   300→            res = self._session.get(url, timeout=10)
   301→            res.raise_for_status()
   302→            remote_doc = res.json()
   303→        except requests.RequestException:
   304→            LOG.info("Profile pull skipped – network unavailable")
   305→            raise
   306→
   307→        remote_profile = UserProfile.from_json(remote_doc)
   308→        with self._lock:
   309→            if remote_profile.last_updated_ts > self._profile.last_updated_ts:
   310→                LOG.debug("Remote profile is newer – updating local copy")
   311→                self._profile = remote_profile
   312→                self._storage.save(remote_profile)
   313→
   314→        # Publish regardless – the event bus de-duplicates on payload hash
   315→        self._bus.publish(Event("profile/updated", payload=remote_profile.to_json()))
   316→        self._notify_listeners(remote_profile)
   317→
   318→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
   319→    # helpers
   320→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
   321→
   322→    def _notify_listeners(self, profile: UserProfile) -> None:
   323→        """Invoke registered callbacks (best-effort, non-blocking)."""
   324→
   325→        def _run(fn: Callable[[UserProfile], None]) -> None:
   326→            try:
   327→                fn(profile)
   328→            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
   329→                LOG.exception("Profile listener raised")
   330→
   331→        for listener in list(self._listeners):  # shallow copy to avoid mutation
   332→            threading.Thread(target=_run, args=(listener,), daemon=True).start()
   333→
   334→    def _initialise_blank_profile(self) -> UserProfile:
   335→        """Create a default profile document for first-time users."""
   336→        LOG.info("Creating new blank profile for user %s", self._user_id)
   337→        profile = UserProfile(
   338→            user_id=self._user_id,
   339→            username=f"user-{self._user_id}",
   340→            preferences={
   341→                "theme": "light",
   342→                "language": "en",
   343→            },
   344→            layout={},
   345→            extra={},
   346→        )
   347→        self._storage.save(profile)
   348→        return profile

pylint: disable=too-many-instance-attributes\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        event_bus: EventBus,\n        user_id: str,\n        *,\n        storage: Optional[StorageProvider] = None,\n        remote_endpoint: str = DEFAULT_CLOUD_ENDPOINT,\n        sync_interval_s: int = 120,\n        max_backoff_s: int = 10 * 60,\n        session: Optional[requests.Session] = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        self._bus = event_bus\n        self._user_id = user_id\n        self._storage = storage or StorageProvider()\n        self._remote_endpoint = remote_endpoint.rstrip(\"/\")\n        self._sync_interval = max(15, sync_interval_s)  # sane lower bound\n        self._max_backoff = max_backoff_s\n        self._session = session or requests.Session()\n        self._lock = threading.RLock()\n\n        self._listeners: List[Callable[[UserProfile], None]] = []\n        self._profile: UserProfile = (\n            self._storage.load(user_id) or self._initialise_blank_profile()\n        )\n\n        self._sync_thread = threading.Thread(\n            target=self._sync_loop,\n            name=\"FlockDeskProfileSync\",\n            daemon=True,\n        )\n        self._stop_event = threading.Event()\n\n        # subscribe to in-process events\n        self._bus.subscribe(\"profile/refresh\", self._on_external_refresh)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # lifecycle\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    def start(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Start the background sync loop.\"\"\"\n        if not self._sync_thread.is_alive():\n            LOG.debug(\"Starting ProfileService sync thread for user %s\", self._user_id)\n            self._sync_thread.start()\n\n    def stop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Signal the background thread to terminate and wait for completion.\"\"\"\n        LOG.debug(\"Stopping ProfileService sync thread for user %s\", self._user_id)\n        self._stop_event.set()\n        self._sync_thread.join(timeout=5)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # public – profile CRUD\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    @property\n    def profile(self) -> UserProfile:\n        with self._lock:\n            return self._profile\n\n    def save(self, profile: UserProfile, *, broadcast: bool = True) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist and broadcast a profile that has been modified in-memory.\"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            profile.last_updated_ts = time.time()\n            self._profile = profile\n            self._storage.save(profile)\n\n        if broadcast:\n            self._bus.publish(Event(\"profile/updated\", payload=profile.to_json()))\n            self._notify_listeners(profile)\n\n    def update_preferences(self, **kwargs: Any) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Convenience helper to modify `preferences`.\"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            self._profile.preferences.update(kwargs)\n            self._profile.last_updated_ts = time.time()\n            self._storage.save(self._profile)\n\n        self._bus.publish(Event(\"profile/updated\", payload=self._profile.to_json()))\n        self._notify_listeners(self._profile)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n    # observer API\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n\n    def register_listener(self, fn: Callable[[UserProfile], None]) -> Callable[[], None]:\n        \"\"\"Register a callback that fires when the profile changes.\n\n        Returns\n        -------\n        Callable[[], None]\n            Unregister function.\n        \"\"\"\n        if fn not in self._listeners:\n            self._listeners.append(fn)\n\n        def _unregister() -> None:\n            try:\n                self._listeners.remove(fn)\n            except ValueError:\n                pass\n\n        return _unregister\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n    # private – event bus hooks\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n\n    def _on_external_refresh(self, event: Event) -> None:  # noqa: D401\n        \"\"\"Handle 'profile/refresh' emitted by external components.\"\"\"\n        LOG.debug(\"Received external profile refresh request event=%s\", event.meta)\n        # Force an immediate remote sync outside of the normal interval\n        threading.Thread(\n            target=self._pull_remote, name=\"FlockDeskProfileForcePull\", daemon=True\n        ).start()\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n    # private – synchronisation loop\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n\n    def _sync_loop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Background loop – push/pull profile with exponential backoff.\"\"\"\n        backoff_s = self._sync_interval\n        while not self._stop_event.is_set():\n            start = time.time()\n            try:\n                self._push_local()    # push first so remote gets our changes\n                self._pull_remote()   # then pull remote updates\n                backoff_s = self._sync_interval  # reset backoff after success\n            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 – catch-all keeps the loop alive\n                LOG.exception(\"Profile sync failed – backing off\")\n                backoff_s = min(backoff_s * 2, self._max_backoff)\n            finally:\n                elapsed = time.time() - start\n                sleep_time = max(backoff_s - elapsed, 1)\n                self._stop_event.wait(timeout=sleep_time)\n\n    def _push_local(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Push local profile to the cloud if it has diverged.\"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            payload = self._profile.to_json()\n\n        url = f\"{self._remote_endpoint}/{self._user_id}\"\n        try:\n            res = self._session.put(url, json=payload, timeout=10)\n            res.raise_for_status()\n            LOG.debug(\"Pushed profile to remote (%s)\", res.status_code)\n        except requests.RequestException:\n            LOG.warning(\"Failed to push profile to cloud\", exc_info=True)\n            raise\n\n    def _pull_remote(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Pull profile from cloud and merge if newer than local.\"\"\"\n        url = f\"{self._remote_endpoint}/{self._user_id}\"\n        try:\n            res = self._session.get(url, timeout=10)\n            res.raise_for_status()\n            remote_doc = res.json()\n        except requests.RequestException:\n            LOG.info(\"Profile pull skipped – network unavailable\")\n            raise\n\n        remote_profile = UserProfile.from_json(remote_doc)\n        with self._lock:\n            if remote_profile.last_updated_ts > self._profile.last_updated_ts:\n                LOG.debug(\"Remote profile is newer – updating local copy\")\n                self._profile = remote_profile\n                self._storage.save(remote_profile)\n\n        # Publish regardless – the event bus de-duplicates on payload hash\n        self._bus.publish(Event(\"profile/updated\", payload=remote_profile.to_json()))\n        self._notify_listeners(remote_profile)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n    # helpers\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n\n    def _notify_listeners(self, profile: UserProfile) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Invoke registered callbacks (best-effort, non-blocking).\"\"\"\n\n        def _run(fn: Callable[[UserProfile], None]) -> None:\n            try:\n                fn(profile)\n            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                LOG.exception(\"Profile listener raised\")\n\n        for listener in list(self._listeners):  # shallow copy to avoid mutation\n            threading.Thread(target=_run, args=(listener,), daemon=True).start()\n\n    def _initialise_blank_profile(self) -> UserProfile:\n        \"\"\"Create a default profile document for first-time users.\"\"\"\n        LOG.info(\"Creating new blank profile for user %s\", self._user_id)\n        profile = UserProfile(\n            user_id=self._user_id,\n            username=f\"user-{self._user_id}\",\n            preferences={\n                \"theme\": \"light\",\n                \"language\": \"en\",\n            },\n            layout={},\n            extra={},\n        )\n        self._storage.save(profile)\n        return profile", "numLines": 348, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 348}}

1→"""
     2→flockdesk.modules.presence.service
     3→=================================
     4→
     5→PresenceService is responsible for tracking the local user’s presence
     6→status, broadcasting updates to the internal event-bus, and consuming
     7→presence-events from other peers so that the rest of the desktop suite
     8→(i.e. chat panes, user lists, dashboards) can present up-to-date
     9→availability information.
    10→
    11→Key responsibilities
    12→--------------------
    13→1. Detect local activity / idleness and derive an appropriate
    14→   `PresenceState`.
    15→2. Expose an explicit `set_status` API so plugins (e.g. “Do-Not-Disturb
    16→   scheduler”) or GUI controls can override the automatic heuristic.
    17→3. Maintain an in-memory registry of *all* known users’ presence data.
    18→4. Publish `presence.updated` events and subscribe to the same topic for
    19→   remote updates—guaranteeing eventual consistency across processes.
    20→5. Provide a coroutine-driven lifecycle that can be started / stopped
    21→   from the application’s service-loader infrastructure.
    22→
    23→The implementation purposely avoids any hard dependency on a concrete
    24→EventBus or IPC layer.  Instead, it relies on a lightweight protocol
    25→interface (`EventBusProtocol`) so that different deployments (embedded
    26→in-proc, ZeroMQ, or an HTTP bridge) can supply their own bus adapter.
    27→"""
    28→
    29→from __future__ import annotations
    30→
    31→import asyncio
    32→import enum
    33→import json
    34→import logging
    35→import sys
    36→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    37→from datetime import datetime, timedelta
    38→from types import TracebackType
    39→from typing import (
    40→    Any,
    41→    Awaitable,
    42→    Callable,
    43→    Dict,
    44→    MutableMapping,
    45→    Optional,
    46→    Protocol,
    47→    Type,
    48→)
    49→
    50→LOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    51→
    52→
    53→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    54→# Protocols & Data Models
    55→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    56→class EventBusProtocol(Protocol):
    57→    """
    58→    A minimal protocol any event-bus implementation has to satisfy so the
    59→    PresenceService can remain transport-agnostic.
    60→    """
    61→
    62→    def subscribe(
    63→        self, topic: str, callback: Callable[[str, Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]]
    64→    ) -> None:  # pragma: no cover
    65→        ...
    66→
    67→    async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:  # noqa: D401
    68→        ...
    69→
    70→
    71→class PresenceState(str, enum.Enum):
    72→    """Enumeration of all presence states supported by FlockDesk."""
    73→
    74→    ONLINE = "online"
    75→    AWAY = "away"
    76→    DND = "dnd"  # Do-Not-Disturb
    77→    OFFLINE = "offline"
    78→
    79→    def __str__(self) -> str:
    80→        return self.value
    81→
    82→
    83→@dataclass(slots=True)
    84→class PresenceInfo:
    85→    """
    86→    Canonical presence payload that gets serialized to the event-bus.
    87→    """
    88→
    89→    user_id: str
    90→    state: PresenceState
    91→    updated_at: datetime = field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)
    92→    # Free-form metadata for plugins (e.g. custom emoji, busy_reason)
    93→    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    94→
    95→    def to_json(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    96→        return {
    97→            "user_id": self.user_id,
    98→            "state": self.state.value,
    99→            "updated_at": self.updated_at.isoformat(),
   100→            "metadata": self.metadata,
   101→        }
   102→
   103→    @classmethod
   104→    def from_json(cls, raw: Dict[str, Any]) -> "PresenceInfo":
   105→        return cls(
   106→            user_id=raw["user_id"],
   107→            state=PresenceState(raw["state"]),
   108→            updated_at=datetime.fromisoformat(raw["updated_at"]),
   109→            metadata=raw.get("metadata", {}),
   110→        )
   111→
   112→
   113→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   114→# Helper Utilities
   115→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   116→def _system_idle_seconds() -> float:
   117→    """
   118→    Cross-platform(ish) API for fetching idle time. We degrade gracefully to
   119→    returning `0` when platform integration is unavailable rather than
   120→    raising—presence detection will still work albeit less smart.
   121→
   122→    Notes
   123→    -----
   124→    • Windows:   GetLastInputInfo via `ctypes`.
   125→    • macOS:     IOKit wrapper (❌ not yet implemented).
   126→    • X11/Wayland:  XScreenSaver (❌ not yet implemented).
   127→    """
   128→    try:
   129→        if sys.platform == "win32":
   130→            import ctypes  # pylint: disable=import-error
   131→
   132→            class LASTINPUTINFO(ctypes.Structure):  # type: ignore
   133→                _fields_ = [("cbSize", ctypes.c_uint), ("dwTime", ctypes.c_uint)]
   134→
   135→            plii = LASTINPUTINFO()
   136→            plii.cbSize = ctypes.sizeof(plii)
   137→            if ctypes.windll.user32.GetLastInputInfo(ctypes.byref(plii)) == 0:  # type: ignore  # noqa
   138→                return 0.0
   139→            millis = ctypes.windll.kernel32.GetTickCount() - plii.dwTime  # type: ignore
   140→            return millis / 1000.0
   141→    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
   142→        LOGGER.debug("Idle time detection failed", exc_info=True)
   143→
   144→    # Unsupported OS or error—fallback.
   145→    return 0.0
   146→
   147→
   148→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   149→# Presence Service
   150→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   151→class PresenceService:
   152→    """
   153→    High-level façade exposed to the rest of the desktop suite.
   154→
   155→    Lifecycle (`async with PresenceService(...):`) is preferred so that the
   156→    activity watchdog coroutine shuts down cleanly during application exit.
   157→    """
   158→
   159→    HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL = timedelta(seconds=30)
   160→    IDLE_THRESHOLD = timedelta(minutes=5)
   161→
   162→    def __init__(
   163→        self,
   164→        *,
   165→        user_id: str,
   166→        bus: EventBusProtocol,
   167→        idle_provider: Callable[[], float] | None = None,
   168→    ) -> None:
   169→        self._user_id: str = user_id
   170→        self._bus = bus
   171→        self._idle_provider = idle_provider or _system_idle_seconds
   172→
   173→        self._registry: MutableMapping[str, PresenceInfo] = {}
   174→        self._local_state: PresenceState = PresenceState.ONLINE
   175→        self._override_state: PresenceState | None = None
   176→
   177→        # Concurrency
   178→        self._loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
   179→        self._tasks: set[asyncio.Task[Any]] = set()
   180→        self._stop_event = asyncio.Event()
   181→
   182→        # Register bus subscription early so we don't miss events during `await __aenter__`
   183→        self._bus.subscribe("presence.updated", self._on_presence_event)
   184→
   185→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   186→    # Context Manager Helpers
   187→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   188→    async def __aenter__(self) -> "PresenceService":
   189→        self.start()
   190→        return self
   191→
   192→    async def __aexit__(
   193→        self,
   194→        exc_type: Type[BaseException] | None,
   195→        exc: BaseException | None,
   196→        tb: TracebackType | None,
   197→    ) -> Optional[bool]:
   198→        await self.stop()
   199→        # Don't suppress any exception
   200→        return None
   201→
   202→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   203→    # Public API
   204→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   205→    def start(self) -> None:
   206→        """Spawn background coroutines (watchdog + heartbeat)."""
   207→        if self._tasks:
   208→            return  # Already started
   209→        LOGGER.debug("PresenceService starting")
   210→        self._tasks.add(self._loop.create_task(self._activity_watchdog()))
   211→        self._tasks.add(self._loop.create_task(self._heartbeat_publisher()))
   212→
   213→    async def stop(self) -> None:
   214→        """Signal background tasks to stop and wait for graceful exit."""
   215→        LOGGER.debug("PresenceService stopping")
   216→        self._stop_event.set()
   217→        # Wait for tasks to finish with timeout
   218→        await asyncio.wait(self._tasks, timeout=2.0)
   219→        self._tasks.clear()
   220→
   221→    async def set_status(
   222→        self,
   223→        state: PresenceState,
   224→        *,
   225→        metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
   226→        override: bool = True,
   227→    ) -> None:
   228→        """
   229→        Explicitly set the local user’s status.
   230→
   231→        Parameters
   232→        ----------
   233→        state:
   234→            One of PresenceState values.
   235→        metadata:
   236→            Optional free-form metadata blob.
   237→        override:
   238→            If `True` automatic idle detection is disabled until
   239→            `clear_manual_override()` is called.  This prevents race
   240→            conditions when the user explicitly picks *DND* but is
   241→            technically “active” according to the heuristic.
   242→        """
   243→        self._override_state = state if override else None
   244→        await self._broadcast_local_state(state, metadata or {})
   245→
   246→    async def clear_manual_override(self) -> None:
   247→        """
   248→        Re-enable automatic presence detection that may have been
   249→        suspended via `set_status(..., override=True)`.
   250→        """
   251→        LOGGER.debug("Presence manual override cleared")
   252→        self._override_state = None
   253→        # Force immediate reconsideration
   254→        await self._evaluate_local_state()
   255→
   256→    def get_presence(self, user_id: str) -> Optional[PresenceInfo]:
   257→        """Return the latest presence data we have for `user_id`."""
   258→        return self._registry.get(user_id)
   259→
   260→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   261→    # Internal – Background Tasks
   262→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   263→    async def _activity_watchdog(self) -> None:
   264→        """
   265→        Periodically check system idle time and update `self._local_state`
   266→        accordingly.
   267→        """
   268→        LOGGER.debug("Presence watchdog started")
   269→        while not self._stop_event.is_set():
   270→            try:
   271→                await self._evaluate_local_state()
   272→            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
   273→                LOGGER.exception("Presence watchdog error")
   274→            await asyncio.sleep(5)  # Poll frequency
   275→
   276→        LOGGER.debug("Presence watchdog terminated")
   277→
   278→    async def _heartbeat_publisher(self) -> None:
   279→        """
   280→        Consolidated heartbeat ensuring that *some* presence update is sent
   281→        at least every `HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL`, even if the state hasn’t
   282→        changed.  This aids recovery in case any bus message was missed.
   283→        """
   284→        LOGGER.debug("Presence heartbeat started")
   285→        while not self._stop_event.is_set():
   286→            try:
   287→                await self._broadcast_local_state(self._local_state, {})
   288→            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
   289→                LOGGER.exception("Presence heartbeat error")
   290→            await asyncio.sleep(self.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL.total_seconds())
   291→
   292→        LOGGER.debug("Presence heartbeat terminated")
   293→
   294→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   295→    # Internal – State Evaluation & Publishing
   296→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   297→    async def _evaluate_local_state(self) -> None:
   298→        """
   299→        Decide which `PresenceState` the local user should be in based on
   300→        idle time and manual overrides.
   301→        """
   302→        # Manual override takes precedence
   303→        if self._override_state:
   304→            state = self._override_state
   305→        else:
   306→            idle_seconds = self._idle_provider()
   307→            state = (
   308→                PresenceState.AWAY
   309→                if idle_seconds >= self.IDLE_THRESHOLD.total_seconds()
   310→                else PresenceState.ONLINE
   311→            )
   312→
   313→        if state != self._local_state:
   314→            LOGGER.debug(
   315→                "Presence state transition: %s → %s (override=%s)",
   316→                self._local_state,
   317→                state,
   318→                bool(self._override_state),
   319→            )
   320→            await self._broadcast_local_state(state, {})
   321→
   322→    async def _broadcast_local_state(
   323→        self, state: PresenceState, metadata: Dict[str, Any]
   324→    ) -> None:
   325→        """
   326→        Update internal registry and publish the change.
   327→        """
   328→        self._local_state = state
   329→        payload = PresenceInfo(
   330→            user_id=self._user_id,
   331→            state=state,
   332→            metadata=metadata,
   333→        )
   334→        # Update local registry synchronously
   335→        self._registry[self._user_id] = payload
   336→        await self._bus.publish("presence.updated", payload.to_json())
   337→
   338→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   339→    # Event-Bus Handlers
   340→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   341→    async def _on_presence_event(self, topic: str, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   342→        """
   343→        Called for *all* presence.updated events—including the one we just
   344→        broadcast ourselves.  We update our registry and forward the
   345→        information to any interested observers (plugins, UI model, etc.).
   346→        """
   347→        try:
   348→            info = PresenceInfo.from_json(data)
   349→        except (ValueError, KeyError) as exc:
   350→            LOGGER.warning("Malformed presence payload: %s (err=%s)", data, exc)
   351→            return
   352→
   353→        # Ignore outdated events – ensure monotonic update per user
   354→        current = self._registry.get(info.user_id)
   355→        if current and info.updated_at <= current.updated_at:
   356→            return
   357→
   358→        self._registry[info.user_id] = info
   359→        LOGGER.debug(
   360→            "Presence updated: %s state=%s meta=%s",
   361→            info.user_id,
   362→            info.state,
   363→            json.dumps(info.metadata, ensure_ascii=False),
   364→        )
   365→
   366→        # Fan-out to local observers by publishing an *internal* asyncio.Event.
   367→        # A more sophisticated implementation might rely on RxPy, signal
   368→        # dispatchers, or other observer patterns.  For now we stick with the
   369→        # event-bus only.
   370→        # NOTE: Nothing to do here; UI layers will subscribe to the same bus
   371→        # topic, or they can query `get_presence()` directly.
   372→

Publish `presence.updated` events and subscribe to the same topic for\n   remote updates—guaranteeing eventual consistency across processes.\n5. Provide a coroutine-driven lifecycle that can be started / stopped\n   from the application’s service-loader infrastructure.\n\nThe implementation purposely avoids any hard dependency on a concrete\nEventBus or IPC layer.  Instead, it relies on a lightweight protocol\ninterface (`EventBusProtocol`) so that different deployments (embedded\nin-proc, ZeroMQ, or an HTTP bridge) can supply their own bus adapter.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport asyncio\nimport enum\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport sys\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime, timedelta\nfrom types import TracebackType\nfrom typing import (\n    Any,\n    Awaitable,\n    Callable,\n    Dict,\n    MutableMapping,\n    Optional,\n    Protocol,\n    Type,\n)\n\nLOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Protocols & Data Models\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nclass EventBusProtocol(Protocol):\n    \"\"\"\n    A minimal protocol any event-bus implementation has to satisfy so the\n    PresenceService can remain transport-agnostic.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def subscribe(\n        self, topic: str, callback: Callable[[str, Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]]\n    ) -> None:  # pragma: no cover\n        ...\n\n    async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:  # noqa: D401\n        ...\n\n\nclass PresenceState(str, enum.Enum):\n    \"\"\"Enumeration of all presence states supported by FlockDesk.\"\"\"\n\n    ONLINE = \"online\"\n    AWAY = \"away\"\n    DND = \"dnd\"  # Do-Not-Disturb\n    OFFLINE = \"offline\"\n\n    def __str__(self) -> str:\n        return self.value\n\n\n@dataclass(slots=True)\nclass PresenceInfo:\n    \"\"\"\n    Canonical presence payload that gets serialized to the event-bus.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    user_id: str\n    state: PresenceState\n    updated_at: datetime = field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)\n    # Free-form metadata for plugins (e.g. custom emoji, busy_reason)\n    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\n\n    def to_json(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        return {\n            \"user_id\": self.user_id,\n            \"state\": self.state.value,\n            \"updated_at\": self.updated_at.isoformat(),\n            \"metadata\": self.metadata,\n        }\n\n    @classmethod\n    def from_json(cls, raw: Dict[str, Any]) -> \"PresenceInfo\":\n        return cls(\n            user_id=raw[\"user_id\"],\n            state=PresenceState(raw[\"state\"]),\n            updated_at=datetime.fromisoformat(raw[\"updated_at\"]),\n            metadata=raw.get(\"metadata\", {}),\n        )\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Helper Utilities\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\ndef _system_idle_seconds() -> float:\n    \"\"\"\n    Cross-platform(ish) API for fetching idle time. We degrade gracefully to\n    returning `0` when platform integration is unavailable rather than\n    raising—presence detection will still work albeit less smart.\n\n    Notes\n    -----\n    • Windows:   GetLastInputInfo via `ctypes`.\n    • macOS:     IOKit wrapper (❌ not yet implemented).\n    • X11/Wayland:  XScreenSaver (❌ not yet implemented).\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        if sys.platform == \"win32\":\n            import ctypes  # pylint: disable=import-error\n\n            class LASTINPUTINFO(ctypes.Structure):  # type: ignore\n                _fields_ = [(\"cbSize\", ctypes.c_uint), (\"dwTime\", ctypes.c_uint)]\n\n            plii = LASTINPUTINFO()\n            plii.cbSize = ctypes.sizeof(plii)\n            if ctypes.windll.user32.GetLastInputInfo(ctypes.byref(plii)) == 0:  # type: ignore  # noqa\n                return 0.0\n            millis = ctypes.windll.kernel32.GetTickCount() - plii.dwTime  # type: ignore\n            return millis / 1000.0\n    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n        LOGGER.debug(\"Idle time detection failed\", exc_info=True)\n\n    # Unsupported OS or error—fallback.\n    return 0.0\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Presence Service\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nclass PresenceService:\n    \"\"\"\n    High-level façade exposed to the rest of the desktop suite.\n\n    Lifecycle (`async with PresenceService(...):`) is preferred so that the\n    activity watchdog coroutine shuts down cleanly during application exit.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL = timedelta(seconds=30)\n    IDLE_THRESHOLD = timedelta(minutes=5)\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        *,\n        user_id: str,\n        bus: EventBusProtocol,\n        idle_provider: Callable[[], float] | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        self._user_id: str = user_id\n        self._bus = bus\n        self._idle_provider = idle_provider or _system_idle_seconds\n\n        self._registry: MutableMapping[str, PresenceInfo] = {}\n        self._local_state: PresenceState = PresenceState.ONLINE\n        self._override_state: PresenceState | None = None\n\n        # Concurrency\n        self._loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()\n        self._tasks: set[asyncio.Task[Any]] = set()\n        self._stop_event = asyncio.Event()\n\n        # Register bus subscription early so we don't miss events during `await __aenter__`\n        self._bus.subscribe(\"presence.updated\", self._on_presence_event)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Context Manager Helpers\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    async def __aenter__(self) -> \"PresenceService\":\n        self.start()\n        return self\n\n    async def __aexit__(\n        self,\n        exc_type: Type[BaseException] | None,\n        exc: BaseException | None,\n        tb: TracebackType | None,\n    ) -> Optional[bool]:\n        await self.stop()\n        # Don't suppress any exception\n        return None\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Public API\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def start(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Spawn background coroutines (watchdog + heartbeat).\"\"\"\n        if self._tasks:\n            return  # Already started\n        LOGGER.debug(\"PresenceService starting\")\n        self._tasks.add(self._loop.create_task(self._activity_watchdog()))\n        self._tasks.add(self._loop.create_task(self._heartbeat_publisher()))\n\n    async def stop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Signal background tasks to stop and wait for graceful exit.\"\"\"\n        LOGGER.debug(\"PresenceService stopping\")\n        self._stop_event.set()\n        # Wait for tasks to finish with timeout\n        await asyncio.wait(self._tasks, timeout=2.0)\n        self._tasks.clear()\n\n    async def set_status(\n        self,\n        state: PresenceState,\n        *,\n        metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,\n        override: bool = True,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Explicitly set the local user’s status.\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        state:\n            One of PresenceState values.\n        metadata:\n            Optional free-form metadata blob.\n        override:\n            If `True` automatic idle detection is disabled until\n            `clear_manual_override()` is called.  This prevents race\n            conditions when the user explicitly picks *DND* but is\n            technically “active” according to the heuristic.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._override_state = state if override else None\n        await self._broadcast_local_state(state, metadata or {})\n\n    async def clear_manual_override(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Re-enable automatic presence detection that may have been\n        suspended via `set_status(..., override=True)`.\n        \"\"\"\n        LOGGER.debug(\"Presence manual override cleared\")\n        self._override_state = None\n        # Force immediate reconsideration\n        await self._evaluate_local_state()\n\n    def get_presence(self, user_id: str) -> Optional[PresenceInfo]:\n        \"\"\"Return the latest presence data we have for `user_id`.\"\"\"\n        return self._registry.get(user_id)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Internal – Background Tasks\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    async def _activity_watchdog(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Periodically check system idle time and update `self._local_state`\n        accordingly.\n        \"\"\"\n        LOGGER.debug(\"Presence watchdog started\")\n        while not self._stop_event.is_set():\n            try:\n                await self._evaluate_local_state()\n            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                LOGGER.exception(\"Presence watchdog error\")\n            await asyncio.sleep(5)  # Poll frequency\n\n        LOGGER.debug(\"Presence watchdog terminated\")\n\n    async def _heartbeat_publisher(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Consolidated heartbeat ensuring that *some* presence update is sent\n        at least every `HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL`, even if the state hasn’t\n        changed.  This aids recovery in case any bus message was missed.\n        \"\"\"\n        LOGGER.debug(\"Presence heartbeat started\")\n        while not self._stop_event.is_set():\n            try:\n                await self._broadcast_local_state(self._local_state, {})\n            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n                LOGGER.exception(\"Presence heartbeat error\")\n            await asyncio.sleep(self.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL.total_seconds())\n\n        LOGGER.debug(\"Presence heartbeat terminated\")\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Internal – State Evaluation & Publishing\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    async def _evaluate_local_state(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Decide which `PresenceState` the local user should be in based on\n        idle time and manual overrides.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Manual override takes precedence\n        if self._override_state:\n            state = self._override_state\n        else:\n            idle_seconds = self._idle_provider()\n            state = (\n                PresenceState.AWAY\n                if idle_seconds >= self.IDLE_THRESHOLD.total_seconds()\n                else PresenceState.ONLINE\n            )\n\n        if state != self._local_state:\n            LOGGER.debug(\n                \"Presence state transition: %s → %s (override=%s)\",\n                self._local_state,\n                state,\n                bool(self._override_state),\n            )\n            await self._broadcast_local_state(state, {})\n\n    async def _broadcast_local_state(\n        self, state: PresenceState, metadata: Dict[str, Any]\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Update internal registry and publish the change.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._local_state = state\n        payload = PresenceInfo(\n            user_id=self._user_id,\n            state=state,\n            metadata=metadata,\n        )\n        # Update local registry synchronously\n        self._registry[self._user_id] = payload\n        await self._bus.publish(\"presence.updated\", payload.to_json())\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Event-Bus Handlers\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    async def _on_presence_event(self, topic: str, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Called for *all* presence.updated events—including the one we just\n        broadcast ourselves.  We update our registry and forward the\n        information to any interested observers (plugins, UI model, etc.).\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            info = PresenceInfo.from_json(data)\n        except (ValueError, KeyError) as exc:\n            LOGGER.warning(\"Malformed presence payload: %s (err=%s)\", data, exc)\n            return\n\n        # Ignore outdated events – ensure monotonic update per user\n        current = self._registry.get(info.user_id)\n        if current and info.updated_at <= current.updated_at:\n            return\n\n        self._registry[info.user_id] = info\n        LOGGER.debug(\n            \"Presence updated: %s state=%s meta=%s\",\n            info.user_id,\n            info.state,\n            json.dumps(info.metadata, ensure_ascii=False),\n        )\n\n        # Fan-out to local observers by publishing an *internal* asyncio.Event.\n        # A more sophisticated implementation might rely on RxPy, signal\n        # dispatchers, or other observer patterns.  For now we stick with the\n        # event-bus only.\n        # NOTE: Nothing to do here; UI layers will subscribe to the same bus\n        # topic, or they can query `get_presence()` directly.\n", "numLines": 372, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 372}}
