   421→                status = PresenceStatus.from_string(status_str)
   422→                last_active = e.get("last_active")
   423→                if isinstance(last_active, str):
   424→                    last_active = _dt.datetime.fromisoformat(last_active)
   425→                else:
   426→                    last_active = _dt.datetime.utcnow()

   428→                entry = PresenceEntry(
   429→                    user_id=e["user_id"],
   430→                    display_name=e.get("display_name", e["user_id"]),
   431→                    status=status,
   432→                    last_active=last_active,
   433→                    avatar_path=e.get("avatar_path"),

   442→    def _on_presence_updated(self, payload):

   445→        # Handle both Event wrapper and direct payload

   453→        try:

   457→            last_active = data.get("updated_at") or data.get("last_active")

   459→                last_active = _dt.datetime.fromisoformat(last_active)
   460→            else:
   461→                last_active = _dt.datetime.utcnow()

   463→            # Get display name from metadata if present (UserService format)
   464→            metadata = data.get("metadata", {})
   465→            display_name = data.get("display_name") or metadata.get("display_name") or data["user_id"]

   468→            entry = PresenceEntry(
   469→                user_id=data["user_id"],
   470→                display_name=display_name,
   471→                status=status,

   479→    # User left – remove from list
   480→    def _on_presence_left(self, payload):
   481→        self._log.debug("presence.left %s", payload)

   489→        self._sync_to_qt(self._model.remove_user, data["user_id"])

   491→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#
   492→    # Cleanup
   493→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#

   495→    def __del__(self):

   502→            task.cancel()

It exposes one public symbol:\n\n    PresenceViewModel  – A Qt QObject that wraps a\n                         QAbstractListModel with presence data and\n                         exposes commands for the UI.\n\nUpdated to use the unified UserService for all user-related data.\n\nAuthor  : FlockDesk Core Team\nLicense : MIT (see project root)\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport asyncio\nimport datetime as _dt\nimport logging\nimport types\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom enum import Enum, auto\nfrom typing import Dict, List, Optional, Any\n\nfrom PySide6 import QtCore\n\n# Import the unified UserService\ntry:\n    from flockdesk.core.services.user_service import UserService, PresenceState as UserPresenceState\nexcept ImportError:\n    UserService = None  # type: ignore\n    UserPresenceState = None  # type: ignore\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# 3rd-party / infrastructure stubs\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# NOTE:\n# The real application injects an actual event-bus implementation that\n# resembles the minimal API below.  We ship a tiny duck-type fallback so\n# the module does not crash when imported in isolation.\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n\nclass _DummySubscription:  # pragma: no cover – testing helper\n    def __init__(self, topic: str, cb):\n        self._topic, self._cb = topic, cb\n\n    def dispose(self):\n        \"\"\"Mimic an un-subscribe operation.\"\"\"\n        logging.getLogger(__name__).debug(\"Unsubscribed from topic %s\", self._topic)\n\n\nclass EventBus:  # pragma: no cover – placeholder\n    \"\"\"Very small subset of the real bus used in production.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self._topics: Dict[str, List] = {}\n\n    def publish(self, topic: str, payload: object) -> None:\n        for cb in self._topics.get(topic, []):\n            try:\n                cb(payload)  # noqa: B023 – sync callback in fake bus\n            except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n                logging.exception(\"Unhandled error in bus subscriber for topic %s\", topic)\n\n    def subscribe(self, topic: str, cb) -> _DummySubscription:\n        self._topics.setdefault(topic, []).append(cb)\n        return _DummySubscription(topic, cb)\n\n\n# When FlockDesk boots, the DI-container injects a **shared** bus\n# instance.  Until then, we fall back to a local private one.\n_default_bus = EventBus()\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# Domain model\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n\nclass PresenceStatus(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Canonical presence states understood by the desktop suite.\"\"\"\n\n    ONLINE = auto()\n    AWAY = auto()\n    BUSY = auto()\n    DND = auto()  # Added DND for compatibility with UserService\n    OFFLINE = auto()\n\n    def __str__(self) -> str:  # Readable for QML\n        return self.name.lower()\n\n    @classmethod\n    def from_string(cls, value: str) -> \"PresenceStatus\":\n        \"\"\"Parse a string status value to PresenceStatus.\"\"\"\n        mapping = {\n            \"online\": cls.ONLINE,\n            \"away\": cls.AWAY,\n            \"busy\": cls.BUSY,\n            \"dnd\": cls.DND,\n            \"do_not_disturb\": cls.DND,\n            \"offline\": cls.OFFLINE,\n        }\n        return mapping.get(value.lower(), cls.OFFLINE)\n\n\n@dataclass(slots=True)\nclass PresenceEntry:\n    user_id: str\n    display_name: str\n    status: PresenceStatus = PresenceStatus.OFFLINE\n    last_active: _dt.datetime = field(default_factory=_dt.datetime.utcnow)\n    avatar_path: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def to_qvariant_map(self) -> Dict[str, object]:\n        \"\"\"Return values convertible by Qt for use in QML/JS.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"userId\": self.user_id,\n            \"displayName\": self.display_name,\n            \"status\": str(self.status),\n            \"lastActive\": self.last_active.isoformat(),\n            \"avatarPath\": self.avatar_path,\n        }\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# QAbstractListModel representing the roster\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n\nclass PresenceListModel(QtCore.QAbstractListModel):\n    \"\"\"\n    Thin wrapper between a list[PresenceEntry] and Qt's model/view API.\n    Emits the usual model signals, hence updates appear instantaneously\n    on bound QML ListViews or QtWidgets views.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    # It exposes one public symbol:\n\n    PresenceViewModel  – A Qt QObject that wraps a\n                         QAbstractListModel with presence data and\n                         exposes commands for the UI.\n\nAuthor  : FlockDesk Core Team\nLicense : MIT (see project root)\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport asyncio\nimport datetime as _dt\nimport logging\nimport types\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom enum import Enum, auto\nfrom typing import Dict, List, Optional\n\nfrom PySide6 import QtCore\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# 3rd-party / infrastructure stubs\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# NOTE:\n# The real application injects an actual event-bus implementation that\n# resembles the minimal API below.  We ship a tiny duck-type fallback so\n# the module does not crash when imported in isolation.\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n\nclass _DummySubscription:  # pragma: no cover – testing helper\n    def __init__(self, topic: str, cb):\n        self._topic, self._cb = topic, cb\n\n    def dispose(self):\n        \"\"\"Mimic an un-subscribe operation.\"\"\"\n        logging.getLogger(__name__).debug(\"Unsubscribed from topic %s\", self._topic)\n\n\nclass EventBus:  # pragma: no cover – placeholder\n    \"\"\"Very small subset of the real bus used in production.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self._topics: Dict[str, List] = {}\n\n    def publish(self, topic: str, payload: object) -> None:\n        for cb in self._topics.get(topic, []):\n            try:\n                cb(payload)  # noqa: B023 – sync callback in fake bus\n            except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n                logging.exception(\"Unhandled error in bus subscriber for topic %s\", topic)\n\n    def subscribe(self, topic: str, cb) -> _DummySubscription:\n        self._topics.setdefault(topic, []).append(cb)\n        return _DummySubscription(topic, cb)\n\n\n# When FlockDesk boots, the DI-container injects a **shared** bus\n# instance.  Until then, we fall back to a local private one.\n_default_bus = EventBus()\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# Domain model\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n\nclass PresenceStatus(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Canonical presence states understood by the desktop suite.\"\"\"\n\n    ONLINE = auto()\n    AWAY = auto()\n    BUSY = auto()\n    OFFLINE = auto()\n\n    def __str__(self) -> str:  # Readable for QML\n        return self.name.lower()\n\n\n@dataclass(slots=True)\nclass PresenceEntry:\n    user_id: str\n    display_name: str\n    status: PresenceStatus = PresenceStatus.OFFLINE\n    last_active: _dt.datetime = field(default_factory=_dt.datetime.utcnow)\n\n    def to_qvariant_map(self) -> Dict[str, object]:\n        \"\"\"Return values convertible by Qt for use in QML/JS.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"userId\": self.user_id,\n            \"displayName\": self.display_name,\n            \"status\": str(self.status),\n            \"lastActive\": self.last_active.isoformat(),\n        }\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# QAbstractListModel representing the roster\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n\nclass PresenceListModel(QtCore.QAbstractListModel):\n    \"\"\"\n    Thin wrapper between a list[PresenceEntry] and Qt's model/view API.\n    Emits the usual model signals, hence updates appear instantaneously\n    on bound QML ListViews or QtWidgets views.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    # Qt Role numeric IDs\n    USER_ID_ROLE = QtCore.Qt.UserRole + 1\n    DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE = USER_ID_ROLE + 1\n    STATUS_ROLE = DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE + 1\n    LAST_ACTIVE_ROLE = STATUS_ROLE + 1\n\n    _ROLE_NAMES = {\n        USER_ID_ROLE: b\"userId\",\n        DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE: b\"displayName\",\n        STATUS_ROLE: b\"status\",\n        LAST_ACTIVE_ROLE: b\"lastActive\",\n    }\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Qt mandatory overrides\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n    def __init__(self, parent: Optional[QtCore.QObject] = None):\n        super().__init__(parent)\n        self._roster: List[PresenceEntry] = []\n\n    # Qt interface – meta\n    def roleNames(self) -> Dict[int, bytes]:  # noqa: N802 – Qt API\n        return self._ROLE_NAMES\n\n    # Qt interface – data\n    def rowCount(self, parent=QtCore.QModelIndex()) -> int:  # noqa: N802\n        return 0 if parent.isValid() else len(self._roster)\n\n    def data(self, index: QtCore.QModelIndex, role: int = QtCore.Qt.DisplayRole):  # noqa: N802\n        if not index.isValid() or not (0 <= index.row() < len(self._roster)):\n            return None\n\n        entry = self._roster[index.row()]\n\n        if role == self.USER_ID_ROLE:\n            return entry.user_id\n        if role == self.DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE:\n            return entry.display_name\n        if role == self.STATUS_ROLE:\n            return str(entry.status)\n        if role == self.LAST_ACTIVE_ROLE:\n            return entry.last_active.isoformat()\n\n        return None\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Mutating helpers exposed to the VM\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n    def upsert_entries(self, entries: List[PresenceEntry]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Insert or update one or many presence rows.\n\n        The algorithm is O(N*M) but roster lists are typically short\n        (max ~200 members).  Optimise later if needed.\n        \"\"\"\n        for entry in entries:\n            try:\n                idx = next(i for i, e in enumerate(self._roster) if e.user_id == entry.user_id)\n                if self._roster[idx] != entry:\n                    self._roster[idx] = entry\n                    top_left = self.index(idx, 0)\n                    self.dataChanged.emit(top_left, top_left, self._ROLE_NAMES.keys())\n            except StopIteration:\n                # New participant – append\n                self.beginInsertRows(QtCore.QModelIndex(), len(self._roster), len(self._roster))\n                self._roster.append(entry)\n                self.endInsertRows()\n\n    def remove_user(self, user_id: str) -> None:\n        try:\n            idx = next(i for i, e in enumerate(self._roster) if e.user_id == user_id)\n        except StopIteration:\n            return  # nothing to do\n        self.beginRemoveRows(QtCore.QModelIndex(), idx, idx)\n        del self._roster[idx]\n        self.endRemoveRows()\n\n    # convenience for QML\n    @QtCore.Slot(str, result=int)\n    def row_for_user(self, user_id: str) -> int:  # noqa: D401\n        \"\"\"Return row index for a given user or ‑1.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return next(i for i, entry in enumerate(self._roster) if entry.user_id == user_id)\n        except StopIteration:\n            return -1\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# Presence View-Model\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n\nclass PresenceViewModel(QtCore.QObject):\n    \"\"\"\n    Qt QObject acting as the binding layer between presence model\n    changes and the GUI.  It subscribes to an *EventBus*, receives\n    events like ``presence.updated`` or ``presence.left``, and updates\n    an internal QAbstractListModel exposed to QML.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    presenceListChanged = QtCore.Signal()  # emitted when the model pointer itself changes\n    errorOccurred = QtCore.Signal(str)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Construction / DI\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        bus: EventBus | None = None,\n        *,\n        current_user_id: Optional[str] = None,\n        parent: Optional[QtCore.QObject] = None,\n    ):\n        super().__init__(parent)\n\n        self._log = logging.getLogger(self.__class__.__name__)\n        self._bus: EventBus = bus or _default_bus\n        self._current_user_id = current_user_id\n\n        # Our roster list-model\n        self._model = PresenceListModel(self)\n\n        # Keep references to bus subscriptions so we can dispose later.\n        self._subscriptions: List[_DummySubscription] = []\n\n        # Async tasks we spawn (cancel on destruction)\n        self._tasks: List[asyncio.Task] = []\n\n        self._wire_bus()\n        self._bootstrap_roster()\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Qt property for QML\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n    @QtCore.Property(QtCore.QObject, notify=presenceListChanged)\n    def presenceModel(self) -> PresenceListModel:  # noqa: D401\n        \"\"\"Return the QAbstractListModel consumed by the view.\"\"\"\n        return self._model\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Public API\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n    @QtCore.Slot(result=str)\n    def currentUserId(self) -> str:  # noqa: D401, N802 – match Qt naming\n        \"\"\"Expose the logged-in user's ID to the view.\"\"\"\n        return self._current_user_id or \"\"\n\n    @QtCore.Slot(str, str)\n    def setStatus(self, user_id: str, raw_status: str) -> None:  # noqa: N802\n        \"\"\"\n        Command from the UI to update somebody's presence manually.\n        Usually only called for *self*.  Publishes an event so the\n        backend broadcasts to everybody else.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            status = PresenceStatus[raw_status.upper()]\n        except KeyError as exc:\n            self.errorOccurred.emit(f\"Invalid status: {raw_status}\")\n            self._log.warning(\"Rejected invalid presence status %s\", raw_status)\n            return\n\n        payload = {\n            \"user_id\": user_id,\n            \"status\": status.name,\n            \"timestamp\": _dt.datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n        }\n        self._bus.publish(\"presence.set_status\", payload)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Private helpers – bus / async\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n    def _wire_bus(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Subscribe to presence events on the internal event-bus.\"\"\"\n        self._subscriptions.append(self._bus.subscribe(\"presence.snapshot\", self._on_snapshot))\n        self._subscriptions.append(self._bus.subscribe(\"presence.updated\", self._on_presence_updated))\n        self._subscriptions.append(self._bus.subscribe(\"presence.left\", self._on_presence_left))\n\n    def _bootstrap_roster(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Ask the backend for the initial roster list via RPC-style\n        request/response.  We use asyncio so as not to block the Qt loop.\n        \"\"\"\n\n        async def request_snapshot():\n            try:\n                # fire-and-wait pattern: publish request, listen for snapshot\n                # The backend replies on the same `presence.snapshot` topic\n                self._bus.publish(\"presence.request_snapshot\", {\"requester\": self._current_user_id})\n                # DONE: nothing, listener will receive snapshot asynchronously\n            except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n                self._log.exception(\"Failed to request presence snapshot\")\n                self.errorOccurred.emit(\"Presence initialisation failed\")\n\n        task = asyncio.create_task(request_snapshot(), name=\"presence.bootstrap\")\n        self._tasks.append(task)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Bus event callbacks – sync context (fake bus).\n    # In production they run on a background thread; we therefore use\n    # Qt's `invokeMethod` to marshal into the main thread.\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n    # The real bus dispatches events on worker threads.  Qt objects are\n    # not thread-safe; we hop back to the main thread via single-shot\n    # queued connections.  `QtCore.QMetaObject.invokeMethod` is used\n    # instead of Signals for performance / testability.\n    def _sync_to_qt(self, fn: types.FunctionType, /, *args):\n        QtCore.QMetaObject.invokeMethod(\n            self,\n            lambda: fn(*args),  # type: ignore[arg-type]  # pyqt signature magic\n            QtCore.Qt.QueuedConnection,\n        )\n\n    # Snapshot – full presence list\n    def _on_snapshot(self, payload):\n        self._log.debug(\"Received presence.snapshot %s\", payload)\n        entries = [\n            PresenceEntry(\n                user_id=e[\"user_id\"],\n                display_name=e[\"display_name\"],\n                status=PresenceStatus[e[\"status\"].upper()],\n                last_active=_dt.datetime.fromisoformat(e[\"last_active\"]),\n            )\n            for e in payload.get(\"members\", [])\n            if \"user_id\" in e\n        ]\n        self._sync_to_qt(self._model.upsert_entries, entries)\n\n    # Incremental updates\n    def _on_presence_updated(self, payload):\n        self._log.debug(\"presence.updated %s\", payload)\n        entry = PresenceEntry(\n            user_id=payload[\"user_id\"],\n            display_name=payload.get(\"display_name\", payload[\"user_id\"]),\n            status=PresenceStatus[payload[\"status\"].upper()],\n            last_active=_dt.datetime.utcnow(),\n        )\n        self._sync_to_qt(self._model.upsert_entries, [entry])\n\n    # User left – remove from list\n    def _on_presence_left(self, payload):\n        self._log.debug(\"presence.left %s\", payload)\n        self._sync_to_qt(self._model.remove_user, payload[\"user_id\"])\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Cleanup\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n    def __del__(self):\n        for sub in self._subscriptions:\n            try:\n                sub.dispose()\n            except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n                self._log.warning(\"Failed to dispose subscription %s\", sub, exc_info=True)\n        for task in self._tasks:\n            task.cancel()"}

1→"""
     2→flockdesk.modules.presence.view.presence_widget
     3→------------------------------------------------
     4→Qt / PySide6 visual component that renders presence information for teammates
     5→inside the FlockDesk desktop suite.
     6→
     7→This widget plays the V (View) part in the MVVM triad.  It is intentionally
     8→*thin*: all business-logic lives in the ViewModel and/or EventBus handlers,
     9→allowing us to hot-swap or unit-test the UI without touching domain code.
    10→
    11→The widget is:
    12→
    13→    • Subscribing to the global EventBus for presence/theme updates.
    14→    • Displaying colleagues in a QListView with custom item-delegates so we
    15→      can paint avatars, names and activity indicators.
    16→    • Drag-and-drop aware – users can drag a colleague into another micro-UI
    17→      (e.g. chat or whiteboard) to open a contextual collaboration pane.
    18→    • Lightweight – starts instantly and survives theme changes without leaks.
    19→
    20→Author:  The FlockDesk team
    21→"""
    22→from __future__ import annotations
    23→
    24→import logging
    25→import sys
    26→from dataclasses import dataclass
    27→from typing import List, Optional
    28→
    29→from PySide6.QtCore import (QAbstractListModel, QByteArray, QModelIndex,
    30→                            QObject, QPoint, Qt, Signal, Slot)
    31→from PySide6.QtGui import (QColor, QDrag, QIcon, QPainter, QPixmap,
    32→                           QStandardItemModel, QTextOption)
    33→from PySide6.QtWidgets import (QApplication, QHBoxLayout, QLabel, QListView,
    34→                               QPushButton, QSpacerItem, QStyle, QStyleOptionViewItem,
    35→                               QStyledItemDelegate, QVBoxLayout, QWidget)
    36→
    37→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    38→# Optional project-local imports                                              #
    39→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    40→
    41→try:
    42→    from flockdesk.core.event_bus import EventBus, Topic  # type: ignore
    43→    from flockdesk.modules.presence.model import PresenceState  # type: ignore
    44→    from flockdesk.shared.theming import ThemeManager  # type: ignore
    45→except (ModuleNotFoundError, ImportError):
    46→    # Fallback stubs to keep linters and RT preview alive
    47→    class EventBus:  # noqa: D401
    48→        """Very small stub when running in isolation."""
    49→        @staticmethod
    50→        def subscribe(topic: str, listener: QObject, slot: str) -> None: ...
    51→        @staticmethod
    52→        def publish(topic: str, payload) -> None: ...
    53→
    54→    class PresenceState:  # noqa: D401
    55→        """Enum stub for online/offline/etc."""
    56→        ONLINE = "online"
    57→        AWAY = "away"
    58→        OFFLINE = "offline"
    59→
    60→    class ThemeManager:  # noqa: D401
    61→        """Simplistic theme stub."""
    62→        theme_changed = Signal(dict)
    63→
    64→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    65→
    66→
    67→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    68→# Dataclasses & Models                                                        #
    69→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    70→
    71→@dataclass(slots=True)
    72→class Colleague:
    73→    """Lightweight data container for a colleague row."""
    74→    uid: str
    75→    display_name: str
    76→    avatar_path: str
    77→    presence: PresenceState
    78→
    79→
    80→class _PresenceListModel(QAbstractListModel):
    81→    """
    82→    A trivial QAbstractListModel that exposes colleagues to the QListView.
    83→
    84→    This model is intentionally 100 % dumb: all mutations come from the
    85→    PresenceViewModel which will feed new lists in one go.
    86→    """
    87→
    88→    # Roles used by the delegate for painting
    89→    UID_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 1
    90→    DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 2
    91→    AVATAR_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 3
    92→    PRESENCE_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 4
    93→
    94→    _ROLE_NAMES = {
    95→        UID_ROLE: QByteArray(b"uid"),
    96→        DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE: QByteArray(b"display_name"),
    97→        AVATAR_ROLE: QByteArray(b"avatar"),
    98→        PRESENCE_ROLE: QByteArray(b"presence"),
    99→    }
   100→
   101→    def __init__(self, colleagues: Optional[List[Colleague]] = None, parent: QObject | None = None) -> None:
   102→        super().__init__(parent)
   103→        self._colleagues: List[Colleague] = colleagues or []
   104→
   105→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   106→    # Required overrides                                                    #
   107→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   108→
   109→    def rowCount(self, parent: QModelIndex = QModelIndex()) -> int:  # noqa: N802
   110→        return 0 if parent.isValid() else len(self._colleagues)
   111→
   112→    def data(self, index: QModelIndex, role: int = Qt.DisplayRole):  # noqa: N802
   113→        if not index.isValid():
   114→            return None
   115→
   116→        try:
   117→            colleague = self._colleagues[index.row()]
   118→        except IndexError:
   119→            return None
   120→
   121→        if role == Qt.DisplayRole:
   122→            return colleague.display_name
   123→        if role == self.UID_ROLE:
   124→            return colleague.uid
   125→        if role == self.DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE:
   126→            return colleague.display_name
   127→        if role == self.AVATAR_ROLE:
   128→            return colleague.avatar_path
   129→        if role == self.PRESENCE_ROLE:
   130→            return colleague.presence
   131→        return None
   132→
   133→    def roleNames(self) -> dict[int, QByteArray]:  # noqa: N802
   134→        return self._ROLE_NAMES
   135→
   136→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   137→    # Public API                                                            #
   138→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   139→
   140→    def update_content(self, colleagues: List[Colleague]) -> None:
   141→        """Replace the entire model dataset in one shot."""
   142→        self.beginResetModel()
   143→        self._colleagues = colleagues
   144→        self.endResetModel()
   145→
   146→
   147→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   148→# Custom Delegate                                                             #
   149→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   150→
   151→class PresenceItemDelegate(QStyledItemDelegate):
   152→    """Paints each list item with avatar, name and presence indicator icon."""
   153→
   154→    AVATAR_SIZE = 32
   155→    PADDING = 6
   156→
   157→    _PRESENCE_ICONS = {
   158→        PresenceState.ONLINE: QIcon(":/presence/online.svg"),
   159→        PresenceState.AWAY: QIcon(":/presence/away.svg"),
   160→        PresenceState.OFFLINE: QIcon(":/presence/offline.svg"),
   161→    }
   162→
   163→    def paint(self, painter: QPainter, option: QStyleOptionViewItem, index: QModelIndex) -> None:
   164→        painter.save()
   165→        try:
   166→            # Draw selection highlight
   167→            if option.state & QStyle.State_Selected:
   168→                painter.fillRect(option.rect, option.palette.highlight())
   169→
   170→            # Fetch data
   171→            name: str = index.data(_PresenceListModel.DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE)
   172→            avatar_path: str = index.data(_PresenceListModel.AVATAR_ROLE)
   173→            presence: PresenceState = index.data(_PresenceListModel.PRESENCE_ROLE)
   174→
   175→            # Avatar
   176→            avatar_rect = option.rect.adjusted(self.PADDING, self.PADDING,
   177→                                               -(option.rect.width() - self.AVATAR_SIZE - self.PADDING),
   178→                                               -self.PADDING)
   179→            avatar_pix = QPixmap(avatar_path).scaled(self.AVATAR_SIZE, self.AVATAR_SIZE,
   180→                                                     Qt.KeepAspectRatio, Qt.SmoothTransformation)
   181→            painter.drawPixmap(avatar_rect.topLeft(), avatar_pix)
   182→
   183→            # Presence indicator bottom-right of avatar
   184→            presence_icon: QIcon = self._PRESENCE_ICONS.get(presence, QIcon())
   185→            indicator_sz = 10
   186→            indicator_rect = avatar_rect.adjusted(self.AVATAR_SIZE - indicator_sz,
   187→                                                  self.AVATAR_SIZE - indicator_sz,
   188→                                                  0, 0)
   189→            presence_pix = presence_icon.pixmap(indicator_sz, indicator_sz)
   190→            painter.drawPixmap(indicator_rect.topLeft(), presence_pix)
   191→
   192→            # User name
   193→            text_rect = option.rect.adjusted(self.AVATAR_SIZE + 2 * self.PADDING,
   194→                                             0, -self.PADDING, 0)
   195→            painter.setPen(option.palette.text().color())
   196→            text_option = QTextOption()
   197→            text_option.setAlignment(Qt.AlignVCenter | Qt.AlignLeft)
   198→            painter.drawText(text_rect, name, text_option)
   199→
   200→        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover
   201→            logger.exception("Failed to paint presence delegate.")
   202→        finally:
   203→            painter.restore()
   204→
   205→    def sizeHint(self, option: QStyleOptionViewItem, index: QModelIndex):  # noqa: N802
   206→        base = super().sizeHint(option, index)
   207→        return base.expandedTo(QSize(0, self.AVATAR_SIZE + 2 * self.PADDING))
   208→
   209→
   210→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   211→# PresenceWidget                                                              #
   212→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   213→
   214→class PresenceWidget(QWidget):
   215→    """
   216→    QWidget exposing team presence.
   217→
   218→    Responsibilities
   219→    ----------------
   220→    1. Build UI (list-view, refresh button)
   221→    2. Bind ViewModel (subscribe to signals / event-bus)
   222→    3. Bridge drag-and-drop to external micro-front-ends
   223→    """
   224→
   225→    # Emitted when the user drags a colleague over somewhere else
   226→    colleagueDragStarted = Signal(str)  # uid
   227→
   228→    def __init__(self, view_model: "PresenceViewModel", parent: QWidget | None = None) -> None:
   229→        super().__init__(parent)
   230→        self._vm = view_model
   231→
   232→        self._build_ui()
   233→        self._bind_view_model()
   234→        self._connect_theme_changes()
   235→
   236→        # Enable drag source
   237→        self._list_view.setDragEnabled(True)
   238→        self._list_view.viewport().setAcceptDrops(False)
   239→        self._list_view.setDefaultDropAction(Qt.IgnoreAction)
   240→
   241→        logger.debug("PresenceWidget initialised.")
   242→
   243→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
   244→    # UI                                                                  #
   245→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
   246→
   247→    def _build_ui(self) -> None:
   248→        self._list_view = QListView(self, objectName="presenceListView")
   249→        self._list_view.setSpacing(4)
   250→        self._list_view.setUniformItemSizes(True)
   251→        self._list_view.setSelectionMode(QListView.NoSelection)
   252→        self._list_view.setItemDelegate(PresenceItemDelegate(self._list_view))
   253→
   254→        self._refresh_btn = QPushButton(self.tr("Refresh"))
   255→        self._refresh_btn.setToolTip(self.tr("Force a manual presence refresh"))
   256→
   257→        # Top bar
   258→        top_layout = QHBoxLayout()
   259→        top_layout.addWidget(QLabel(self.tr("Presence")))
   260→        top_layout.addItem(QSpacerItem(0, 0, QSizePolicy.Expanding, QSizePolicy.Minimum))
   261→        top_layout.addWidget(self._refresh_btn)
   262→
   263→        # Main layout
   264→        layout = QVBoxLayout(self)
   265→        layout.addLayout(top_layout)
   266→        layout.addWidget(self._list_view)
   267→        self.setLayout(layout)
   268→
   269→        # Model
   270→        self._model = _PresenceListModel([])
   271→        self._list_view.setModel(self._model)
   272→
   273→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
   274→    # Data Binding                                                       #
   275→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
   276→
   277→    def _bind_view_model(self) -> None:
   278→        self._refresh_btn.clicked.connect(self._vm.refresh)
   279→        self._vm.presence_changed.connect(self._on_presence_changed)
   280→
   281→        # For initial data
   282→        self._on_presence_changed(self._vm.snapshot())
   283→
   284→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
   285→    # Theme                                                              #
   286→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
   287→
   288→    def _connect_theme_changes(self) -> None:
   289→        if hasattr(ThemeManager, "theme_changed"):
   290→            ThemeManager.theme_changed.connect(self._on_theme_changed)
   291→
   292→    @Slot(dict)
   293→    def _on_theme_changed(self, theme_meta: dict) -> None:
   294→        """
   295→        Triggered when the global theme changed.
   296→
   297→        For the sake of demo we only update the list spacing/palette; more
   298→        thorough implementation would adapt colors in the delegate, etc.
   299→        """
   300→        palette = self.palette()
   301→        palette.setColor(self.backgroundRole(), QColor(theme_meta.get("window", "#FFFFFF")))
   302→        self.setPalette(palette)
   303→
   304→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
   305→    # ViewModel slot                                                     #
   306→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
   307→
   308→    @Slot(list)
   309→    def _on_presence_changed(self, colleagues: List[Colleague]) -> None:
   310→        logger.debug("PresenceWidget received %d colleagues.", len(colleagues))
   311→        self._model.update_content(colleagues)
   312→
   313→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
   314→    # Drag-and-drop                                                      #
   315→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
   316→
   317→    def startDrag(self, supportedActions: Qt.DropActions) -> None:  # noqa: N802
   318→        """Override QListView drag start behaviour."""
   319→        index = self._list_view.currentIndex()
   320→        if not index.isValid():
   321→            return
   322→
   323→        uid = index.data(_PresenceListModel.UID_ROLE)
   324→        display_name = index.data(_PresenceListModel.DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE)
   325→        avatar_path = index.data(_PresenceListModel.AVATAR_ROLE)
   326→
   327→        logger.debug("Start drag for user %s", display_name)
   328→        drag = QDrag(self)
   329→        mime_data = self._vm.build_mime_data(uid)
   330→        drag.setMimeData(mime_data)
   331→
   332→        # Pixmap for drag feedback
   333→        pixmap = QPixmap(avatar_path).scaled(48, 48, Qt.KeepAspectRatio, Qt.SmoothTransformation)
   334→        drag.setPixmap(pixmap)
   335→        drag.setHotSpot(QPoint(pixmap.width() // 2, pixmap.height() // 2))
   336→
   337→        drag.exec_(Qt.MoveAction)
   338→        self.colleagueDragStarted.emit(uid)
   339→
   340→
   341→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   342→# ViewModel interface placeholder                                             #
   343→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   344→
   345→class PresenceViewModel(QObject):
   346→    """
   347→    This interface is implemented elsewhere in the presence module.
   348→
   349→    PresenceWidget only relies on the signals described below.
   350→    """
   351→
   352→    presence_changed = Signal(list)  # List[Colleague]
   353→
   354→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
   355→    # API expected by the widget                                         #
   356→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
   357→
   358→    def refresh(self) -> None: ...  # noqa: D400
   359→
   360→    def snapshot(self) -> List[Colleague]:
   361→        """Return current presence list synchronously."""
   362→        return []
   363→
   364→    def build_mime_data(self, uid: str) -> "QMimeData": ...  # noqa: D401
   365→
   366→
   367→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   368→# Debug entry-point (can be stripped for prod)                                #
   369→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   370→
   371→if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover
   372→    # Minimal harness to showcase the widget in isolation.
   373→    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)
   374→
   375→    class _DummyVM(PresenceViewModel):
   376→
   377→        def __init__(self) -> None:
   378→            super().__init__()
   379→            self._colleagues = [
   380→                Colleague("1", "Alice", ":/avatars/alice.png", PresenceState.ONLINE),
   381→                Colleague("2", "Bob", ":/avatars/bob.png", PresenceState.AWAY),
   382→                Colleague("3", "Charlie", ":/avatars/charlie.png", PresenceState.OFFLINE),
   383→            ]
   384→
   385→        def refresh(self) -> None:
   386→            logger.info("Refresh called from UI")
   387→            # In real case we might ask server; here we just toggle Bob
   388→            import random
   389→            self._colleagues[1].presence = random.choice(
   390→                [PresenceState.ONLINE, PresenceState.AWAY, PresenceState.OFFLINE])
   391→            self.presence_changed.emit(self._colleagues)
   392→
   393→        def snapshot(self) -> List[Colleague]:
   394→            return self._colleagues
   395→
   396→        def build_mime_data(self, uid: str):
   397→            from PySide6.QtCore import QMimeData
   398→            md = QMimeData()
   399→            md.setText(uid)
   400→            return md
   401→
   402→    app = QApplication(sys.argv)
   403→    w = PresenceWidget(_DummyVM())
   404→    w.resize(240, 320)
   405→    w.show()
   406→    sys.exit(app.exec())

arguments.content:
"""
flockdesk.modules.presence.view.presence_widget
------------------------------------------------
Qt / PySide6 visual component that renders presence information for teammates
inside the FlockDesk desktop suite.

This widget plays the V (View) part in the MVVM triad.  It is intentionally
*thin*: all business-logic lives in the ViewModel and/or EventBus handlers,
allowing us to hot-swap or unit-test the UI without touching domain code.

The widget is:

    • Subscribing to the global EventBus for presence/theme updates.
    • Displaying colleagues in a QListView with custom item-delegates so we
      can paint avatars, names and activity indicators.
    • Drag-and-drop aware – users can drag a colleague into another micro-UI
      (e.g. chat or whiteboard) to open a contextual collaboration pane.
    • Lightweight – starts instantly and survives theme changes without leaks.

Updated to optionally use the unified UserService for user data.

Author:  The FlockDesk team
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import sys
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import List, Optional, TYPE_CHECKING

from PySide6.QtCore import (QAbstractListModel, QByteArray, QModelIndex,
                            QObject, QPoint, Qt, Signal, Slot, QSize)
from PySide6.QtGui import (QColor, QDrag, QIcon, QPainter, QPixmap,
                           QStandardItemModel, QTextOption)
from PySide6.QtWidgets import (QApplication, QHBoxLayout, QLabel, QListView,
                               QPushButton, QSpacerItem, QStyle, QStyleOptionViewItem,
                               QStyledItemDelegate, QVBoxLayout, QWidget, QSizePolicy)

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# Optional project-local imports                                              #
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

try:
    from flockdesk.core.event_bus import EventBus, Topic  # type: ignore
    from flockdesk.modules.presence.model import PresenceState  # type: ignore
    from flockdesk.shared.theming import ThemeManager  # type: ignore
except (ModuleNotFoundError, ImportError):
    # Fallback stubs to keep linters and RT preview alive
    class EventBus:  # noqa: D401
        """Very small stub when running in isolation."""
        @staticmethod
        def subscribe(topic: str, listener: QObject, slot: str) -> None: ...
        @staticmethod
        def publish(topic: str, payload) -> None: ...

    class PresenceState:  # noqa: D401
        """Enum stub for online/offline/etc."""
        ONLINE = "online"
        AWAY = "away"
        DND = "dnd"
        OFFLINE = "offline"

    class ThemeManager:  # noqa: D401
        """Simplistic theme stub."""
        theme_changed = Signal(dict)

# Import UserService if available
try:
    from flockdesk.core.services.user_service import UserService
except ImportError:
    UserService = None  # type: ignore

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# Dataclasses & Models                                                        #
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

@dataclass(slots=True)
class Colleague:
    """Lightweight data container for a colleague row."""
    uid: str
    display_name: str
    avatar_path: str
    presence: str  # Changed from PresenceState to str for flexibility

    @classmethod
    def from_user_view(cls, user_view: dict) -> "Colleague":
        """Create a Colleague from a UserService user view."""
        return cls(
            uid=user_view["user_id"],
            display_name=user_view.get("display_name", user_view["user_id"]),
            avatar_path=user_view.get("avatar_path", ""),
            presence=user_view.get("status", "offline"),
        )


class _PresenceListModel(QAbstractListModel):
    """
    A trivial QAbstractListModel that exposes colleagues to the QListView.

    This model is intentionally 100 % dumb: all mutations come from the
    PresenceViewModel which will feed new lists in one go.
    """

    # Roles used by the delegate for painting
    UID_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 1
    DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 2
    AVATAR_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 3
    PRESENCE_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 4

    _ROLE_NAMES = {
        UID_ROLE: QByteArray(b"uid"),
        DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE: QByteArray(b"display_name"),
        AVATAR_ROLE: QByteArray(b"avatar"),
        PRESENCE_ROLE: QByteArray(b"presence"),
    }

    def __init__(self, colleagues: Optional[List[Colleague]] = None, parent: QObject | None = None) -> None:
        super().__init__(parent)
        self._colleagues: List[Colleague] = colleagues or []

    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    # Required overrides                                                    #
    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #

    def rowCount(self, parent: QModelIndex = QModelIndex()) -> int:  # noqa: N802
        return 0 if parent.isValid() else len(self._colleagues)

    def data(self, index: QModelIndex, role: int = Qt.DisplayRole):  # noqa: N802
        if not index.isValid():
            return None

        try:
            colleague = self._colleagues[index.row()]
        except IndexError:
            return None

        if role == Qt.DisplayRole:
            return colleague.display_name
        if role == self.UID_ROLE:
            return colleague.uid
        if role == self.DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE:
            return colleague.display_name
        if role == self.AVATAR_ROLE:
            return colleague.avatar_path
        if role == self.PRESENCE_ROLE:
            return colleague.presence
        return None

    def roleNames(self) -> dict[int, QByteArray]:  # noqa: N802
        return self._ROLE_NAMES

    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    # Public API                                                            #
    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #

    def update_content(self, colleagues: List[Colleague]) -> None:
        """Replace the entire model dataset in one shot."""
        self.beginResetModel()
        self._colleagues = colleagues
        self.endResetModel()


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# Custom Delegate                                                             #
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

class PresenceItemDelegate(QStyledItemDelegate):
    """Paints each list item with avatar, name and presence indicator icon."""

    AVATAR_SIZE = 32
    PADDING = 6

    # Map string status values to icons
    _PRESENCE_ICONS = {
        "online": QIcon(":/presence/online.svg"),
        "away": QIcon(":/presence/away.svg"),
        "dnd": QIcon(":/presence/dnd.svg"),
        "offline": QIcon(":/presence/offline.svg"),
    }

    # Also support enum-like objects
    @classmethod
    def _get_presence_icon(cls, presence) -> QIcon:
        """Get icon for a presence state (string or enum)."""
        if isinstance(presence, str):
            return cls._PRESENCE_ICONS.get(presence.lower(), QIcon())
        elif hasattr(presence, 'value'):
            return cls._PRESENCE_ICONS.get(str(presence.value).lower(), QIcon())
        else:
            return cls._PRESENCE_ICONS.get(str(presence).lower(), QIcon())

    def paint(self, painter: QPainter, option: QStyleOptionViewItem, index: QModelIndex) -> None:
        painter.save()
        try:
            # Draw selection highlight
            if option.state & QStyle.State_Selected:
                painter.fillRect(option.rect, option.palette.highlight())

            # Fetch data
            name: str = index.data(_PresenceListModel.DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE)
            avatar_path: str = index.data(_PresenceListModel.AVATAR_ROLE)
            presence = index.data(_PresenceListModel.PRESENCE_ROLE)

            # Avatar
            avatar_rect = option.rect.adjusted(self.PADDING, self.PADDING,
                                               -(option.rect.width() - self.AVATAR_SIZE - self.PADDING),
                                               -self.PADDING)
            if avatar_path:
                avatar_pix = QPixmap(avatar_path).scaled(self.AVATAR_SIZE, self.AVATAR_SIZE,
                                                         Qt.KeepAspectRatio, Qt.SmoothTransformation)
                painter.drawPixmap(avatar_rect.topLeft(), avatar_pix)

            # Presence indicator bottom-right of avatar
            presence_icon = self._get_presence_icon(presence)
            indicator_sz = 10
            indicator_rect = avatar_rect.adjusted(self.AVATAR_SIZE - indicator_sz,
                                                  self.AVATAR_SIZE - indicator_sz,
                                                  0, 0)
            presence_pix = presence_icon.pixmap(indicator_sz, indicator_sz)
            painter.drawPixmap(indicator_rect.topLeft(), presence_pix)

            # User name
            text_rect = option.rect.adjusted(self.AVATAR_SIZE + 2 * self.PADDING,
                                             0, -self.PADDING, 0)
            painter.setPen(option.palette.text().color())
            text_option = QTextOption()
            text_option.setAlignment(Qt.AlignVCenter | Qt.AlignLeft)
            painter.drawText(text_rect, name or "", text_option)

        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover
            logger.exception("Failed to paint presence delegate.")
        finally:
            painter.restore()

    def sizeHint(self, option: QStyleOptionViewItem, index: QModelIndex):  # noqa: N802
        base = super().sizeHint(option, index)
        return base.expandedTo(QSize(0, self.AVATAR_SIZE + 2 * self.PADDING))


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# PresenceWidget                                                              #
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

class PresenceWidget(QWidget):
    """
    QWidget exposing team presence.

    Responsibilities
    ----------------
    1. Build UI (list-view, refresh button)
    2. Bind ViewModel (subscribe to signals / event-bus)
    3. Bridge drag-and-drop to external micro-front-ends
    4. Optionally integrate with UserService for unified user data
    """

    # Emitted when the user drags a colleague over somewhere else
    colleagueDragStarted = Signal(str)  # uid

    def __init__(
        self,
        view_model: "PresenceViewModel",
        user_service: Optional["UserService"] = None,
        parent: QWidget | None = None
    ) -> None:
        super().__init__(parent)
        self._vm = view_model
        self._user_service = user_service

        self._build_ui()
        self._bind_view_model()
        self._connect_theme_changes()

        # Enable drag source
        self._list_view.setDragEnabled(True)
        self._list_view.viewport().setAcceptDrops(False)
        self._list_view.setDefaultDropAction(Qt.IgnoreAction)

        logger.debug("PresenceWidget initialised.")

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # UI                                                                  #
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #

    def _build_ui(self) -> None:
        self._list_view = QListView(self, objectName="presenceListView")
        self._list_view.setSpacing(4)
        self._list_view.setUniformItemSizes(True)
        self._list_view.setSelectionMode(QListView.NoSelection)
        self._list_view.setItemDelegate(PresenceItemDelegate(self._list_view))

        self._refresh_btn = QPushButton(self.tr("Refresh"))
        self._refresh_btn.setToolTip(self.tr("Force a manual presence refresh"))

        # Top bar
        top_layout = QHBoxLayout()
        top_layout.addWidget(QLabel(self.tr("Presence")))
        top_layout.addItem(QSpacerItem(0, 0, QSizePolicy.Expanding, QSizePolicy.Minimum))
        top_layout.addWidget(self._refresh_btn)

        # Main layout
        layout = QVBoxLayout(self)
        layout.addLayout(top_layout)
        layout.addWidget(self._list_view)
        self.setLayout(layout)

        # Model
        self._model = _PresenceListModel([])
        self._list_view.setModel(self._model)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Data Binding                                                       #
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #

    def _bind_view_model(self) -> None:
        self._refresh_btn.clicked.connect(self._vm.refresh)
        self._vm.presence_changed.connect(self._on_presence_changed)

        # For initial data - try UserService first, fall back to snapshot
        if self._user_service:
            self._refresh_from_user_service()
        else:
            self._on_presence_changed(self._vm.snapshot())

    def _refresh_from_user_service(self) -> None:
        """Refresh colleague list from UserService."""
        if not self._user_service:
            return

        colleagues = []
        for user_view in self._user_service.get_all_users():
            colleagues.append(Colleague.from_user_view(user_view))
        self._model.update_content(colleagues)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Theme                                                              #
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #

    def _connect_theme_changes(self) -> None:
        if hasattr(ThemeManager, "theme_changed"):
            ThemeManager.theme_changed.connect(self._on_theme_changed)

    @Slot(dict)
    def _on_theme_changed(self, theme_meta: dict) -> None:
        """
        Triggered when the global theme changed.

        For the sake of demo we only update the list spacing/palette; more
        thorough implementation would adapt colors in the delegate, etc.
        """
        palette = self.palette()
        palette.setColor(self.backgroundRole(), QColor(theme_meta.get("window", "#FFFFFF")))
        self.setPalette(palette)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # ViewModel slot                                                     #
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #

    @Slot(list)
    def _on_presence_changed(self, colleagues: List[Colleague]) -> None:
        logger.debug("PresenceWidget received %d colleagues.", len(colleagues))
        self._model.update_content(colleagues)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Drag-and-drop                                                      #
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #

    def startDrag(self, supportedActions: Qt.DropActions) -> None:  # noqa: N802
        """Override QListView drag start behaviour."""
        index = self._list_view.currentIndex()
        if not index.isValid():
            return

        uid = index.data(_PresenceListModel.UID_ROLE)
        display_name = index.data(_PresenceListModel.DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE)
        avatar_path = index.data(_PresenceListModel.AVATAR_ROLE)

        logger.debug("Start drag for user %s", display_name)
        drag = QDrag(self)
        mime_data = self._vm.build_mime_data(uid)
        drag.setMimeData(mime_data)

        # Pixmap for drag feedback
        if avatar_path:
            pixmap = QPixmap(avatar_path).scaled(48, 48, Qt.KeepAspectRatio, Qt.SmoothTransformation)
            drag.setPixmap(pixmap)
            drag.setHotSpot(QPoint(pixmap.width() // 2, pixmap.height() // 2))

        drag.exec_(Qt.MoveAction)
        self.colleagueDragStarted.emit(uid)

# ViewModel interface placeholder                                             #
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

    """

    """

    presence_changed = Signal(list)  # List[Colleague]

        super().__init__()
        self._user_service = user_service

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #

        if self._user_service:

            self.presence_changed.emit(colleagues)

    def snapshot(self) -> List[Colleague]:
        """Return current presence list synchronously."""
        if self._user_service:
            colleagues = []
            for user_view in self._user_service.get_all_users():
                colleagues.append(Colleague.from_user_view(user_view))
            return colleagues
        return []

    def build_mime_data(self, uid: str) -> "QMimeData": ...  # noqa: D401

# Debug entry-point (can be stripped for prod)                                #
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover
    # Minimal harness to showcase the widget in isolation.
    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)

    class _DummyVM(PresenceViewModel):

        def __init__(self) -> None:
            super().__init__()
            self._colleagues = [
                Colleague("1", "Alice", ":/avatars/alice.png", "online"),
                Colleague("2", "Bob", ":/avatars/bob.png", "away"),
                Colleague("3", "Charlie", ":/avatars/charlie.png", "offline"),
            ]

        def refresh(self) -> None:
            logger.info("Refresh called from UI")
            # In real case we might ask server; here we just toggle Bob
            import random
            statuses = ["online", "away", "dnd", "offline"]
            self._colleagues[1].presence = random.choice(statuses)
            self.presence_changed.emit(self._colleagues)

        def snapshot(self) -> List[Colleague]:
            return self._colleagues

        def build_mime_data(self, uid: str):

            md.setText(uid)
            return md

    app = QApplication(sys.argv)
    w = PresenceWidget(_DummyVM())
    w.resize(240, 320)

input.content:
"""
flockdesk.modules.presence.view.presence_widget
------------------------------------------------
Qt / PySide6 visual component that renders presence information for teammates
inside the FlockDesk desktop suite.

This widget plays the V (View) part in the MVVM triad.  It is intentionally
*thin*: all business-logic lives in the ViewModel and/or EventBus handlers,
allowing us to hot-swap or unit-test the UI without touching domain code.

The widget is:

    • Subscribing to the global EventBus for presence/theme updates.
    • Displaying colleagues in a QListView with custom item-delegates so we
      can paint avatars, names and activity indicators.
    • Drag-and-drop aware – users can drag a colleague into another micro-UI
      (e.g. chat or whiteboard) to open a contextual collaboration pane.
    • Lightweight – starts instantly and survives theme changes without leaks.

Updated to optionally use the unified UserService for user data.

Author:  The FlockDesk team
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import sys
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import List, Optional, TYPE_CHECKING

from PySide6.QtCore import (QAbstractListModel, QByteArray, QModelIndex,
                            QObject, QPoint, Qt, Signal, Slot, QSize)
from PySide6.QtGui import (QColor, QDrag, QIcon, QPainter, QPixmap,
                           QStandardItemModel, QTextOption)
from PySide6.QtWidgets import (QApplication, QHBoxLayout, QLabel, QListView,
                               QPushButton, QSpacerItem, QStyle, QStyleOptionViewItem,
                               QStyledItemDelegate, QVBoxLayout, QWidget, QSizePolicy)

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# Optional project-local imports                                              #
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

try:
    from flockdesk.core.event_bus import EventBus, Topic  # type: ignore
    from flockdesk.modules.presence.model import PresenceState  # type: ignore
    from flockdesk.shared.theming import ThemeManager  # type: ignore
except (ModuleNotFoundError, ImportError):
    # Fallback stubs to keep linters and RT preview alive
    class EventBus:  # noqa: D401
        """Very small stub when running in isolation."""
        @staticmethod
        def subscribe(topic: str, listener: QObject, slot: str) -> None: ...
        @staticmethod
        def publish(topic: str, payload) -> None: ...

    class PresenceState:  # noqa: D401
        """Enum stub for online/offline/etc."""
        ONLINE = "online"
        AWAY = "away"
        DND = "dnd"
        OFFLINE = "offline"

    class ThemeManager:  # noqa: D401
        """Simplistic theme stub."""
        theme_changed = Signal(dict)

# Import UserService if available
try:
    from flockdesk.core.services.user_service import UserService
except ImportError:
    UserService = None  # type: ignore

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# Dataclasses & Models                                                        #
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

@dataclass(slots=True)
class Colleague:
    """Lightweight data container for a colleague row."""
    uid: str
    display_name: str
    avatar_path: str
    presence: str  # Changed from PresenceState to str for flexibility

    @classmethod
    def from_user_view(cls, user_view: dict) -> "Colleague":
        """Create a Colleague from a UserService user view."""
        return cls(
            uid=user_view["user_id"],
            display_name=user_view.get("display_name", user_view["user_id"]),
            avatar_path=user_view.get("avatar_path", ""),
            presence=user_view.get("status", "offline"),
        )


class _PresenceListModel(QAbstractListModel):
    """
    A trivial QAbstractListModel that exposes colleagues to the QListView.

    This model is intentionally 100 % dumb: all mutations come from the
    PresenceViewModel which will feed new lists in one go.
    """

    # Roles used by the delegate for painting
    UID_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 1
    DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 2
    AVATAR_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 3
    PRESENCE_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 4

    _ROLE_NAMES = {
        UID_ROLE: QByteArray(b"uid"),
        DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE: QByteArray(b"display_name"),
        AVATAR_ROLE: QByteArray(b"avatar"),
        PRESENCE_ROLE: QByteArray(b"presence"),
    }

    def __init__(self, colleagues: Optional[List[Colleague]] = None, parent: QObject | None = None) -> None:
        super().__init__(parent)
        self._colleagues: List[Colleague] = colleagues or []

    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    # Required overrides                                                    #
    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #

    def rowCount(self, parent: QModelIndex = QModelIndex()) -> int:  # noqa: N802
        return 0 if parent.isValid() else len(self._colleagues)

    def data(self, index: QModelIndex, role: int = Qt.DisplayRole):  # noqa: N802
        if not index.isValid():
            return None

        try:
            colleague = self._colleagues[index.row()]
        except IndexError:
            return None

        if role == Qt.DisplayRole:
            return colleague.display_name
        if role == self.UID_ROLE:
            return colleague.uid
        if role == self.DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE:
            return colleague.display_name
        if role == self.AVATAR_ROLE:
            return colleague.avatar_path
        if role == self.PRESENCE_ROLE:
            return colleague.presence
        return None

    def roleNames(self) -> dict[int, QByteArray]:  # noqa: N802
        return self._ROLE_NAMES

    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    # Public API                                                            #
    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #

    def update_content(self, colleagues: List[Colleague]) -> None:
        """Replace the entire model dataset in one shot."""
        self.beginResetModel()
        self._colleagues = colleagues
        self.endResetModel()


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# Custom Delegate                                                             #
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

class PresenceItemDelegate(QStyledItemDelegate):
    """Paints each list item with avatar, name and presence indicator icon."""

    AVATAR_SIZE = 32
    PADDING = 6

    # Map string status values to icons
    _PRESENCE_ICONS = {
        "online": QIcon(":/presence/online.svg"),
        "away": QIcon(":/presence/away.svg"),
        "dnd": QIcon(":/presence/dnd.svg"),
        "offline": QIcon(":/presence/offline.svg"),
    }

    # Also support enum-like objects
    @classmethod
    def _get_presence_icon(cls, presence) -> QIcon:
        """Get icon for a presence state (string or enum)."""
        if isinstance(presence, str):
            return cls._PRESENCE_ICONS.get(presence.lower(), QIcon())
        elif hasattr(presence, 'value'):
            return cls._PRESENCE_ICONS.get(str(presence.value).lower(), QIcon())
        else:
            return cls._PRESENCE_ICONS.get(str(presence).lower(), QIcon())

    def paint(self, painter: QPainter, option: QStyleOptionViewItem, index: QModelIndex) -> None:
        painter.save()
        try:
            # Draw selection highlight
            if option.state & QStyle.State_Selected:
                painter.fillRect(option.rect, option.palette.highlight())

            # Fetch data
            name: str = index.data(_PresenceListModel.DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE)
            avatar_path: str = index.data(_PresenceListModel.AVATAR_ROLE)
            presence = index.data(_PresenceListModel.PRESENCE_ROLE)

            # Avatar
            avatar_rect = option.rect.adjusted(self.PADDING, self.PADDING,
                                               -(option.rect.width() - self.AVATAR_SIZE - self.PADDING),
                                               -self.PADDING)
            if avatar_path:
                avatar_pix = QPixmap(avatar_path).scaled(self.AVATAR_SIZE, self.AVATAR_SIZE,
                                                         Qt.KeepAspectRatio, Qt.SmoothTransformation)
                painter.drawPixmap(avatar_rect.topLeft(), avatar_pix)

            # Presence indicator bottom-right of avatar
            presence_icon = self._get_presence_icon(presence)
            indicator_sz = 10
            indicator_rect = avatar_rect.adjusted(self.AVATAR_SIZE - indicator_sz,
                                                  self.AVATAR_SIZE - indicator_sz,
                                                  0, 0)
            presence_pix = presence_icon.pixmap(indicator_sz, indicator_sz)
            painter.drawPixmap(indicator_rect.topLeft(), presence_pix)

            # User name
            text_rect = option.rect.adjusted(self.AVATAR_SIZE + 2 * self.PADDING,
                                             0, -self.PADDING, 0)
            painter.setPen(option.palette.text().color())
            text_option = QTextOption()
            text_option.setAlignment(Qt.AlignVCenter | Qt.AlignLeft)
            painter.drawText(text_rect, name or "", text_option)

        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover
            logger.exception("Failed to paint presence delegate.")
        finally:
            painter.restore()

    def sizeHint(self, option: QStyleOptionViewItem, index: QModelIndex):  # noqa: N802
        base = super().sizeHint(option, index)
        return base.expandedTo(QSize(0, self.AVATAR_SIZE + 2 * self.PADDING))


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# PresenceWidget                                                              #
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

class PresenceWidget(QWidget):
    """
    QWidget exposing team presence.

    Responsibilities
    ----------------
    1. Build UI (list-view, refresh button)
    2. Bind ViewModel (subscribe to signals / event-bus)
    3. Bridge drag-and-drop to external micro-front-ends
    4. Optionally integrate with UserService for unified user data
    """

    # Emitted when the user drags a colleague over somewhere else
    colleagueDragStarted = Signal(str)  # uid

    def __init__(
        self,
        view_model: "PresenceViewModel",
        user_service: Optional["UserService"] = None,
        parent: QWidget | None = None
    ) -> None:
        super().__init__(parent)
        self._vm = view_model
        self._user_service = user_service

        self._build_ui()
        self._bind_view_model()
        self._connect_theme_changes()

        # Enable drag source
        self._list_view.setDragEnabled(True)
        self._list_view.viewport().setAcceptDrops(False)
        self._list_view.setDefaultDropAction(Qt.IgnoreAction)

        logger.debug("PresenceWidget initialised.")

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # UI                                                                  #
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #

    def _build_ui(self) -> None:
        self._list_view = QListView(self, objectName="presenceListView")
        self._list_view.setSpacing(4)
        self._list_view.setUniformItemSizes(True)
        self._list_view.setSelectionMode(QListView.NoSelection)
        self._list_view.setItemDelegate(PresenceItemDelegate(self._list_view))

        self._refresh_btn = QPushButton(self.tr("Refresh"))
        self._refresh_btn.setToolTip(self.tr("Force a manual presence refresh"))

        # Top bar
        top_layout = QHBoxLayout()
        top_layout.addWidget(QLabel(self.tr("Presence")))
        top_layout.addItem(QSpacerItem(0, 0, QSizePolicy.Expanding, QSizePolicy.Minimum))
        top_layout.addWidget(self._refresh_btn)

        # Main layout
        layout = QVBoxLayout(self)
        layout.addLayout(top_layout)
        layout.addWidget(self._list_view)
        self.setLayout(layout)

        # Model
        self._model = _PresenceListModel([])
        self._list_view.setModel(self._model)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Data Binding                                                       #
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #

    def _bind_view_model(self) -> None:
        self._refresh_btn.clicked.connect(self._vm.refresh)
        self._vm.presence_changed.connect(self._on_presence_changed)

        # For initial data - try UserService first, fall back to snapshot
        if self._user_service:
            self._refresh_from_user_service()
        else:
            self._on_presence_changed(self._vm.snapshot())

    def _refresh_from_user_service(self) -> None:
        """Refresh colleague list from UserService."""
        if not self._user_service:
            return

        colleagues = []
        for user_view in self._user_service.get_all_users():
            colleagues.append(Colleague.from_user_view(user_view))
        self._model.update_content(colleagues)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Theme                                                              #
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #

    def _connect_theme_changes(self) -> None:
        if hasattr(ThemeManager, "theme_changed"):
            ThemeManager.theme_changed.connect(self._on_theme_changed)

    @Slot(dict)
    def _on_theme_changed(self, theme_meta: dict) -> None:
        """
        Triggered when the global theme changed.

        For the sake of demo we only update the list spacing/palette; more
        thorough implementation would adapt colors in the delegate, etc.
        """
        palette = self.palette()
        palette.setColor(self.backgroundRole(), QColor(theme_meta.get("window", "#FFFFFF")))
        self.setPalette(palette)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # ViewModel slot                                                     #
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #

    @Slot(list)
    def _on_presence_changed(self, colleagues: List[Colleague]) -> None:
        logger.debug("PresenceWidget received %d colleagues.", len(colleagues))
        self._model.update_content(colleagues)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Drag-and-drop                                                      #
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #

    def startDrag(self, supportedActions: Qt.DropActions) -> None:  # noqa: N802
        """Override QListView drag start behaviour."""
        index = self._list_view.currentIndex()
        if not index.isValid():
            return

        uid = index.data(_PresenceListModel.UID_ROLE)
        display_name = index.data(_PresenceListModel.DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE)
        avatar_path = index.data(_PresenceListModel.AVATAR_ROLE)

        logger.debug("Start drag for user %s", display_name)
        drag = QDrag(self)
        mime_data = self._vm.build_mime_data(uid)
        drag.setMimeData(mime_data)

        # Pixmap for drag feedback
        if avatar_path:
            pixmap = QPixmap(avatar_path).scaled(48, 48, Qt.KeepAspectRatio, Qt.SmoothTransformation)
            drag.setPixmap(pixmap)
            drag.setHotSpot(QPoint(pixmap.width() // 2, pixmap.height() // 2))

        drag.exec_(Qt.MoveAction)
        self.colleagueDragStarted.emit(uid)


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# ViewModel interface placeholder                                             #
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

class PresenceViewModel(QObject):
    """
    This interface is implemented elsewhere in the presence module.

    PresenceWidget only relies on the signals described below.

    Updated to optionally use UserService.
    """

    presence_changed = Signal(list)  # List[Colleague]

    def __init__(self, user_service: Optional["UserService"] = None) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        self._user_service = user_service

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # API expected by the widget                                         #
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #

    def refresh(self) -> None:
        """Refresh presence data."""
        if self._user_service:
            # Trigger a refresh from UserService
            colleagues = []
            for user_view in self._user_service.get_all_users():
                colleagues.append(Colleague.from_user_view(user_view))
            self.presence_changed.emit(colleagues)

    def snapshot(self) -> List[Colleague]:
        """Return current presence list synchronously."""
        if self._user_service:
            colleagues = []
            for user_view in self._user_service.get_all_users():
                colleagues.append(Colleague.from_user_view(user_view))
            return colleagues
        return []

    def build_mime_data(self, uid: str) -> "QMimeData": ...  # noqa: D401


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# Debug entry-point (can be stripped for prod)                                #
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover
    # Minimal harness to showcase the widget in isolation.
    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)

    class _DummyVM(PresenceViewModel):

        def __init__(self) -> None:
            super().__init__()
            self._colleagues = [
                Colleague("1", "Alice", ":/avatars/alice.png", "online"),
                Colleague("2", "Bob", ":/avatars/bob.png", "away"),
                Colleague("3", "Charlie", ":/avatars/charlie.png", "offline"),
            ]

        def refresh(self) -> None:
            logger.info("Refresh called from UI")
            # In real case we might ask server; here we just toggle Bob
            import random
            statuses = ["online", "away", "dnd", "offline"]
            self._colleagues[1].presence = random.choice(statuses)
            self.presence_changed.emit(self._colleagues)

        def snapshot(self) -> List[Colleague]:
            return self._colleagues

        def build_mime_data(self, uid: str):
            from PySide6.QtCore import QMimeData
            md = QMimeData()
            md.setText(uid)
            return md

    app = QApplication(sys.argv)
    w = PresenceWidget(_DummyVM())
    w.resize(240, 320)
    w.show()
    sys.exit(app.exec())

    80→@dataclass(slots=True)
    81→class Colleague:

    84→    display_name: str
    85→    avatar_path: str

    87→

    93→            display_name=user_view.get("display_name", user_view["user_id"]),
